programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo investigates a murder at the military academy he once attended, after a murdered marine and alumni of the school is found clutching a photo of a current student. Meanwhile, Bishop and her husband invite the team out for a special dinner. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects Gibbs's arch-nemesis is behind an explosion at a summit on global terror. Meanwhile, a grief-stricken Fornell struggles to deal with the fatal shooting of his ex-wife. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Joe Spano

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: When poachers injure an endangered and pregnant deer, Team Scorpion must recreate the animal's womb to save both lives

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the case of a marine who dropped dead while scaling a wall on an assault course, and a fellow soldier reveals that he was heavily involved with an underground fight club, where competitors would battle for cash. Meanwhile, having returned to work after compassionate leave, Vance discovers paperwork and a key in his late wife's effects that lead him to a safety deposit box containing a stash of cash

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. The agents realise he may have been killed in error, and that the real target was his ex-girlfriend, a skilled player of computer games who unwittingly uncovered a mysterious code. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents learn one of their former investigative assistants has been murdered, and had been selling information about how to rob a warehouse full of valuable possessions belonging to a woman convicted of fraud. As the case proceeds, Tony becomes infatuated with the new leader of another team, who apparently returns his feelings. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The attendees at a swanky tech convention, including Walter and Paige, are taken hostage by a group attempting a virtual heist. Sly tries to ace a big exam.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team investigate the existence of ghosts on the Queen Mary, but soon find themselves dealing with real disaster as they try to prevent a deadly collision.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A couple are taking in the view from a cliff top when a car driven by an already bleeding man crashes into theirs and teeters on the brink before plunging over the precipice. Callen and the team are brought in to investigate when the driver is identified as Lieutenant Brian Roth, and they discover that the car is registered to a Hollywood socialite Aubrey Darva. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey find their glamorous new jobs in a casino have a hidden downside, while Dorien rekindles an old flame

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: As the crucial final match approaches, Fisher asks Crabbe to keep an eye on the police football ground and look after an ex-professional player with a murky past - but there may be more to his new protege than meets the eye. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: A teenage apprentice starts work in the restaurant kitchens. At first, he seems a decent, hard-working lad, but then disappears - along with the takings from a busy evening. Richard Griffiths and Kevin Bishop star

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Sean Bean returns as Bernard Cornwell's swashbuckling hero, this time setting out to rescue British deserters captured by Spanish rebel El Casco. Along the way he crosses swords with a fellow officer and come to the aid of a damsel in distress. Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and John Tams also star

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: After a 10-year-old girl is injured in a petrol bomb attack by animal rights activists, an outraged Gene takes Alex to interview a veteran campaigner in jail. Though the prisoner is weak and incoherent after going on hunger strike, Alex is convinced he not only has details of planned attacks - he may also know about her own situation and be able to help her get home. Guest starring David Bradley and Rory Kinnear

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene receives a visit from Jackie Queen, a journalist from Manchester, who announces to the shocked superintendent she is pregnant with his baby. She is also investigating the disappearance of teenagers suspected of running away to London, so the team launches an investigation and a suspect is quickly identified. However, it becomes clear the man is being protected by someone within the Met. John Bowe and Ruth Millar guest star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A schoolgirl's killing brings Gently into the alien world of pop and media celebrity when it turns out the victim's best friend is a rising TV star. Bacchus suspects the dead girl's music teacher, since rumours persist that she was having an affair with him. But when it seems everyone has a different opinion of the girl, Gently must uncover these different faces to get to the truth of her murder. Neil Morrissey guest stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A couple stumble upon the body of an elderly man in the old hospital wing of an abandoned mental health facility, strapped to a gurney with electrodes connected to his temples

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The Fisher household acquires a new wireless receiver, while Dot realises that she doesn't want to relinquish working for Phryne when she marries. Australian crime drama, starring Essie Davis and Ashleigh Cummings

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The unveiling of a reconstructed dinosaur skeleton at a gala event leads to a murder inquiry after the dead body of a young man is found in its jaws. The detective finds himself embroiled in the world of bone-hunters as he attempts to track down the culprit. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective encounters escape artist Harry Houdini when he finds himself investigating a daring bank robbery involving the murder of one of its guards, while Julia's sister Ruby pays Toronto a visit and soon becomes attracted to the sleuth. Yannick Bisson stars

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective investigates a failed rape attempt by a masked attacker known as the Bowman. However, his job is complicated when the victim commits suicide and the criminal strikes again. Glasgow-set detective drama, with Mark McManus

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing monk defends a man accused of murdering a wealthy and powerful baron, with only a mysterious leper as his ally. Medieval drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver and Sarah Badel

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack and Soca Officer Marcus Swift follow the false lead and prepare to corner the drugs gang at Jamaica Dock, while newcomer Honey is being held prisoner at Bonham Wharf boatyard, hoping in vain for help from her colleagues. Haskew finally convinces Kristen that Zain really is an undercover cop, and Jack is horrified to learn his detective has been sleeping with the sultry club owner

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Friday night shift proves a hectic time for the Sun Hill coppers as a childhood friend of Amber's is raped, a drug-dealer is collared and a mugging victim is attacked for a second time - and in the aftermath of all the excitement, Phil's unruly hormones are fired by an encounter with a fellow sex addict. A guilt-ridden Honey decides to tell Steve their relationship is over, Neil pours out his heart to Andrea, and Yvonne learns she's no longer Acting Sergeant - leading to much speculation about her replacement

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gina reports Honey as a missing person when she fails to report for work and it's not long before her fingerprints are found at Bonham Wharf, identifying her as the second prisoner at the scene. Zain panics as the net closes on him, but Kristen remains calm and reassures him they can make a clean getaway. John Heaton's wife tells him they are through and Emma finds out just how controlling her new husband can be

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Sun Hill watch are briefed about Honey's death and Will is embarrassed to learn her mother wants to meet him as she has heard so much about him. Zain comes under intense questioning from the Murder Investigation Team but manages to give Phil and Neil the slip in order to meet up with Kristen and make his escape

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Man Who Cried
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama about Abel Mason, an ordinary working man whose extramarital affair leads to murder, and prompts him to hit the road with his son in search of a new life. The pair head north and Abel eventually finds employment with a wealthy garage owner. Ciaran Hinds and Kate Buffery star

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Gambling Man
programme.desc: Robson Green stars in the title role as Rory Connor, a rent collector who spends his nights at the gaming tables, secure in the knowledge that nobody can turn a card quite like him. Unfortunately, as the stakes get higher, Connor finds himself gambling more than money - with dramatic consequences for himself and those around him. Feature-length drama, with Bernard Hill and Sylvestra Le Touzel

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A Christian Scientist couple refuse medicine for their newborn baby, leaving Sister Julienne questioning the nature of absolute faith. However, the case becomes more complicated when the parents are accused of harming their child. Barbara uses her initiative to communicate with a pregnant Sylheti woman who cannot speak English, although using the patient's young son to translate provides new problems. Sister Mary Cynthia returns to Nonnatus House and a woman catches handyman Fred's eye

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Paris stands on the brink of revolt during the funeral of General Lamarque. Enjolras leads a group of protesting students and workers to construct a barricade in the streets, while an increasingly desperate Valjean fears for Cosette's safety and arranges for her to go into hiding as Javert's search for them grows ever more relentless. Marius scours the city for the missing Cosette, assisted by a heartbroken Eponine - but when he cannot find her, he is left consumed with despair and decides to join Enjolras at the forefront of the uprising

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Two further bodies and a skeleton are found at the former boarding house and Walker isn't convinced that the alibi of former guest Graham Richards is enough

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The past begins to catch up with 12th-century sleuth Brother Cadfael as he investigates the case of a wealthy landowner murdered shortly after disinheriting his son. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Sean Pertwee, Peter Copley, Sophie Lawrence and Julian Firth

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: An elderly Sarah reveals the truth about Bert Ford's death. Meanwhile in 1953, Sarah and Anna start their trial periods in their new homes, and Jack is furious at Carolyn for having kept their daughter a secret. Olivia becomes concerned over James's brutal hospital treatment and Elizabeth makes a shock announcement. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon tries to climb the social ladder - but ends up meeting a snake. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A student at Rod's school is reluctant to talk about who wounded him with a knife, but some dogged routine work by Reg and Diane searching through CCTV footage eventually gives the team a lead, linking the incident to the disappearance of a paroled prisoner last seen stealing bandages from a chemist's shop. The sight of Gina in her glad rags prompts June to wish for early retirement - and an early wedding. Leslie Grantham guest stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Michael pleads with Donna to lie for him as Annalese pursues the truth about his fidelity. Linden finds evidence of Jac's scheming but struggles to decide what to do, while Chrissie tries to get Daisha out of Mark's life. Ric's zero-tolerance programme is challenged by the offer of a charitable donation to the hospital, and Elliot's decision to bring his dog to work again backfires when it knocks Jac over. Melanie Hill and David Roper guest star

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Abs tries to get through to an increasingly unstable foster child who has harmed herself with a broken bottle because of her fear of turning out like her alcoholic mother. Charlie's first custody hearing is adjourned after Louis discovers his grandad fighting for breath, and Comfort agrees to Fin's suggestion that they see a relationship counsellor, while Lara shares a kiss with Simon on his return to work

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy's wish for a quiet birthday is soon forgotten when Kezia announces she's got a party planned and he finds himself dodging bullets on the Aldbourne estate. Newly promoted DI Sam Nixon is put firmly in her place by Neil and promptly goes behind his back to revive the search for missing youngster Amy Tennant on the anniversary of her disappearance. June tells Tony and Reg she's taking early retirement

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle has some unfinished business to attend to, Cindy has to do some quick thinking but soon realises she hasn't been rumbled, and Arthur has a welcome surprise

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The past catches up with Ted, Pauline crosses swords with Michelle, and Roy is out for Barry's blood

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Regina threatens Sarah's future as George's life hangs in the balance, while Anna and Gino's honeymoon is clouded by the contents of Swanson's letter

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Tom fears cyclist Nick Pullen is being pushed beyond his limits by an overzealous trainer, while Will clashes with ex-wife Kate over an alcoholic patient _ who leaves him with a bloody nose. Sam's mounting debts tempt him to defraud the partnership as his wedding day draws near. Gray O'Brien and Simon Shepherd star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Herriot household becomes inceasingly hectic, Tristan contemplates putting his bachelor days behind him, and Siegfried's new assistant arrives - bringing his badger with him. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A new season begins, but Jeffrey appears to have vanished. Classic comedy, starring Ruth Madoc and David Griffin

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: With no sign of Jeffrey Fairbrother, Ted is summoned to Head Office, and jumps to the conclusion that he is about to be made entertainments manager. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth forces Richard to participate in a trip to the countryside to look for Iron Age remains, but further cultural excursions are curtailed when Daisy and Onslow report that Rose is behaving in an outrageous fashion. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler finds himself pursued by a wanton gold-digger who thinks he's rich, but luckily Truly is full of ideas to solve the problem. Stephen Lewis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to create a restaurant garden on Italy's Ligurian coast, but it is not long before their working holiday is disrupted by a murder. Determined to solve the crime before returning home, they find themselves with no shortage of suspects - and matters are complicated further by a second death. Guest starring Suzanne Bertish (Rome) and Tristan Gemmill (Casualty), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are revealed, putting Strike and Robin in danger as they close in on Lula's killer. Robin receives key information from a salesgirl at the boutique Lula visited, Strike asks Bristow about Lula's attempts to trace her biological roots and the impact on her adoptive family, while John points out that he, Tony and Yvette all have an alibi. However, Strike manages to unravel Tony's alibi as the truth about Lula's death is finally revealed. Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and James Bolam play Redman's veteran team, with Jon Finch, Tim Woodwork and Philip Bretherton

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The team races to stop a CIA plan to assassinate the Venezuelan president in Britain in the hope of gaining intelligence about an expected al-Qaeda attack on a school. Having identified the group of mercenaries that kidnapped Zaf, Jo too is snatched by them. Adam's attempts to gain her freedom result in a high-level conspiracy being exposed - and more tragedy for the Section D officers

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

This time, contestants Matthew Waterhouse, Louise Jameson, David Graham, Sophia Myles, Jimmy Vee, Lauren Socha, Madeline Smith and Stephanie Beacham take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Lee and Cariad from south Wales, brothers Dion and Kuresh from London, and twins from Liverpool Cathy and Sharon

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the eight remaining couples return to the Strictly Ballroom in a special episode paying tribute to all things Blackpool. programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. Unfortunately, he didn't take into account the Queen's tendency to change her mind. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Newcastle and Chelsea were both looking to emulate impressive victories in last season's corresponding encounters

programme.title: The NFL Show

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Lifeline

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a quirky terraced house in Birmingham, a flat in a Glasgow high rise block, and a former taxi cab office in north London

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Fulham v Everton (Kick-off 12.00pm). Coverage of the Premier League match from Craven Cottage, where the Cottagers play host to the Toffees. Scott Parker's side have found life difficult on their return to the top flight, picking up just four points from their opening eight games, and will enter this fixture as underdogs despite being the home side. Everton were one of the early season pace-setters but entered the recent international break having lost three games in a row, and will be keen to return to winning ways after such a promising start to the campaign

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins hosts the girls' semi-final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year, with five youngsters hoping to impress judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams scour the Royal Cornwall Showground for items to sell for profit at auction, assisted by experts Caroline Hawley and Ben Cooper. Presented by Eric Knowles

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset, and also visits Dunkeswell Aerodrome

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Witley, Surrey, where she rescues two tip-bound items that she thinks she can turn into a profit for the people getting rid of them

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live in volcanic hot spots on the ocean bed, areas that reach temperatures hot enough to melt lead and which may hold the secrets behind all life on Earth. Other strange creatures from the depths of the sea include bone-eating worms, fish that walk rather than swim, and hordes of squid. Plus, footage of sharks in a feeding frenzy over the dead body of a whale and the wildlife of the Midnight Zone in the Pacific, an area that never receives any natural light, forcing creatures to rely on their own bioluminescence

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley are in the Lincolnshire wolds at historic South Ormsby, which has ambitions to be the finest farming estate in the world. They discover the entrepreneurial spirit driving the revival of the local rural economy, including two young women carving out new careers. Elsewhere, Adam Henson has high hopes for an inner-city teenage jockey competing in her first race, while Tom Heap investigates whether it's time to ditch the green belt

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the fourth pair to leave the contest. With music by Billy Ocean, who performs a medley of his hits

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra loses the alethiometer after accepting a lift from Latrom, while Lee Scoresby has an altercation with the strange Dr Haley at the observatory where Grumman used to work and is arrested. In the North, Iorek tells Kaisa that Lyra went through the anomaly, and Mrs Coulter's airship is redirected to Yenisei. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Drama focusing on the romantic reggae genre and to the black youth who found freedom and love in its sound in London house parties, when they were unwelcome in white nightclubs. Drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, who also directed, starring Micheal Ward and Amarah-Jae St Aubyn

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Sheffield United v West Ham United at Bramall Lane and Leeds United v Arsenal at Elland Road. The Blades were looking to emulate their 1-0 victory over West Ham in last season's corresponding fixture. Leeds and Arsenal met at Emirates Stadium in the third round of last season's FA Cup, when the Gunners won 1-0, but United demonstrated their ability to be competitive against top-flight teams. Plus, a round-up of the rest of the weekend's goals

programme.title: Bury: Bringing Football Back
programme.desc: A look at the attempts of a group of locals to bring football back to Bury after the local football club was expelled from the English Football League in August 2019. The film follows Chris Murray and his friends over the course of 12 months, getting behind-the-scenes access to the birth of a new football club and seeing what it really takes to make dreams a reality

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing gold medallist Katherine Grainger, former England striker Dion Dublin and Great Britain 400m greats Christine Ohuruogu and Iwan Thomas join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin head to properties in Chesterton in Staffordshire, Hornchurch in Essex and Sudbrook in Monmouthshire to hear the buyers' plans

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Questions are asked when a year after a house fire a second claim comes in, while the data embedded in digital photographs submitted to support stolen items does not add up

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers who toil through the small hours to get the world ready for when the rest of the population awakes. In the first edition, vets perform potentially life-saving surgery on a much-loved horse, and bus cleaners work to have Newcastle's transport ready for commuters

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks are in Leominster, Herefordshire, to help two teams look for items, while presenter Christina Trevanion learns about local photographer Derek Evans

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al comes up with a plan to get out of the engagement, but Lily has some news of her own. Jimmi is called to the scene of a death and soon finds it has far-reaching consequences

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple searching for a house in rural North Yorkshire on a budget of £800,000, and also visits Harrogate for afternoon tea and to sample the region's traditional sweet treats

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph salvages three items from Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey, then she and two experts transform four dining chairs, a pair of wardrobe doors and a retro cabinet

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell takes on Tim Medhurst as they search for items on either side of the border between England and Scotland on their way to an auction in Edinburgh

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set about transforming a sea of dingy gravel and paving for a couple eager for a peaceful haven. Owners Andrew and Dawn also want a design that allows the family caravan to be accommodated. Whose design will fall on stony ground?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A surprise call from Lauren in New Zealand leaves Peter asking his dad for financial help - can Ian redeem himself? Linda is surprised to find Mick at home nursing a bruised eye, while he tries to cover his displeasure when Max talks about helping her with her new business venture. Kheerat warns Tina about moving in with Gray, claming he is not who people think he is

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. Her arrival causes consternation among the locals - particularly stuffy councillor David Horton, who vows to have her ejected at all costs. Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers co-star

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Journalist Josh Baker investigates the story of former Indiana resident Samantha Sally and her family. After arriving in Syria, her husband became an Islamic State sniper and her young son Matthew was forced to take part in an infamous propaganda video. Sally, who is now in prison, describes her life with the terror group and the part she played in buying sex slaves

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff of Longlox are reunited as they prepare to re-open the salon after lockdown, and while things are going well with Gary, Sammyjo is feeling the pressure to get everything in place for her waiting clients

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The salon opens for its first weekend after lockdown, Gary pays busy Sammyjo a visit, and sad news at home affects Holly's replacement

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Richard Osman and Fin Taylor joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hugh Grant discusses his starring role in psychological thriller The Undoing, while TV cook and food writer Nigella Lawson talks about her new book Cook, Eat, Repeat. Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey reflect on their involvement in new Disney animation Soul and six-time Oscar-nominated Amy Adams talks about starring in the latest film from Ron Howard, Hillbilly Elegy. Plus, Dua Lipa performs Levitating

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood discover some uninvited animal guests, including a herd of elephants in a hotel, and an ant that indulges in some shocking behaviour

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and company discover a carrot cake that does not contain any carrots in Singapore, and explore the hilltop city of Shimla, where the British Raj spent the Indian summer

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A young boy called Aron loves all things Bollywood, especially dancing, so the team builds him his very own Bollywood-style den

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Online superstar and CBBC presenter Nikki Lilly is in the studio to demonstrate some amazing life hacks from her new book, there is a look back at Lindsey's epic Children in Need challenge and the presenters attempt a dance challenge

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve looks back at the most awesome experiments over the series and chooses his highlights. He then uses them to build the ultimate deadly dino and create a crazy hybrid

programme.title: The Big Life Fix with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Designers Ryan White and Zoe Laughlin explore if there is anything they can do to help 11-year-old Malachi, who has Tourette's syndrome and ADHD, control his tics and behavioural issues. Plus, a challenge to get a four-year-old who is paralysed from the waist down walking again, and helping a quadriplegic man use his mobile and credit card when he is out and about without having to ask for help

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles between homemade fighting robots, including a towering, sword-wielding warrior built by a Mensa member and his three sons, that is one of the tallest machines to ever enter the arena and runs on wheels that are the height of a washing machine. Other contestants include a mechanical lobster and a 19-year-old self-taught engineer's creation, which has teeth that spin at 9000RPM. The conflict takes a dramatic turn when one of the robots bursts into flames

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of first-time puppy owners. Rescue dog Elina moves in with the Orchard family in Buckinghamshire after a 2,000-mile trip from Cyprus, where she was found abandoned on the side of the road. Meanwhile, Sage the Staffordshire bull terrier cross is a massive hit with the Fianko family

programme.title: Live Women's International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v France (Kick-off 12.00pm). Lee McKenzie presents all the action from the autumn international at Twickenham. The sides also met in France last weekend, but regardless of that outcome, England will be expecting to prevail on this occasion given their dominant record against their opponents on home soil. With commentary by Sara Orchard

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in rural Somerset with two sport enthusiasts who have a £1million budget and want to buy a country home with views and a garden for football

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An insight into the life of David Lean, whose drive and genius made him one of Britain's greatest ever film directors. Featuring a compilation of rarely seen interviews and archive footage. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made by actor Noel Coward, featuring archive interview clips showing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Brief Encounter (1945)
programme.desc: A suburban housewife meets a married doctor by chance in a railway station waiting room. Later, their paths cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. David Lean's romantic drama, starring Celia Johnson and Trevor Howard

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom visits the Black Bull in the Stirlingshire village of Gartmore. Struggling to survive, it was put on the market and 250 locals clubbed together to buy it. Now the pub is staffed entirely by volunteers and the management are planning to renovate the bedrooms to attract tourists. But Tom has his concerns. He also catches up with the staff of the pubs he is helping in Cornwall, south London and Stroud - but just when all the hard work seems to be paying off, the pandemic strikes. Now every pub in Britain faces a fight for survival

programme.title: The River: A Year in the Life of the Tay
programme.desc: Writer and naturalist Helen Macdonald examines life along the River Tay, following the river's course from Ben Lui in the West to Dundee in the East, encountering salmon, beavers and the microscopic creatures and learning about the history of the Britain's largest river

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of 'radio activity', with Joe Lycett, Shazia Mirza, Josh Widdicombe and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Fela Kuti: Father of Afrobeat
programme.desc: The story of the influential African musician, who is remembered not only as the creator of the Afrobeat genre but also as an outspoken political revolutionary who criticised military regimes in his native Nigeria. A counter-cultural revolutionary, he fought injustice with his music and provoked society with his lifestyle, on one occasion marrying 27 wives in a single ceremony. When he died in 1997 from HIV, a disease that carried huge stigma in Africa, there was fear his legacy would die with him, but this film shows that the impact of his life and music is still felt today

programme.title: Lynn + Lucy (2019)
programme.desc: Having grown up together, young mothers Lynn and Lucy are inseparable - neighbours and best friends, they are as close as can be. But when tragedy befalls the pair, a chain of events is set in motion that severely tests their bond. Mystery drama, starring Roxanne Scrimshaw, Nichola Burley and Kacey Ainsworth

programme.title: Sus (2010)
programme.desc: A black man is taken in for questioning when his wife is found murdered on the night of the 1979 general election. He comes to realise that the police have already assumed he killed her, while the racist detectives interrogating him use increasingly brutal methods to force him into a false confession. Drama, with Clint Dyer, Ralph Brown, Rafe Spall and Anjela Lauren Smith

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter T, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden embarks on a journey to explore some of the most impressive gardens in the UK - from a hot-air balloon. In the first edition, she hovers above Cornwall, and finds herself overwhelmed with nostalgia by the giant Brazilian gunnera. Later, she meets a man whose childhood was spent playing in the garden, before testing her nerves at St Michael's Mount where she encounters 'gardening on the edge'

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: The horticulturist presents a year-long diary about the life cycle of her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon, where she offers seasonal advice and guidance, beginning with how to look after plants throughout January and February. She prunes the greenery, prepares the soil and plants garlic, before a local woodsman helps her lay a native hedge

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to the Isle of Rum where the community is looking for new people to come and live there, having constructed eco-homes for new families. Euan McIlwraith follows in the footsteps of some of the islanders from St Kilda who were evacuated exactly 90 years ago and made a new life on the mainland. Anne Lundon investigates Roan island's unique medical experiments, while over on Luing off the Argyll coast Arlene Stuart finds out about the famous cattle breed developed on the island

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Visiting gardens that encourage and work alongside wildlife, featuring some unique wildlife footage from badgers to butterflies

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook travels to Gower to meet and eat with a little-known community of incomers enjoying life on one of the country's most beautiful coastlines. At the heart of the community are the Blytt-Jordens - three generations of Welsh-Norwegians who are among several such clans settled in the area who have only recently discovered each other. In Michela's company, they rustle up traditional Norwegian delicacies from national pudding tilsorte bondepiker to hot waffles and brown cheese and fermented fish. They also get a chance to chat in their first language

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers revisit the 1970s, a decade which is all too often unfairly stigmatised for its food. They show how a well-prepared chicken cordon bleu and banoffee pie can be a contemporary gastronomic delight, before heading to a restaurant whose menu harks back to that era

programme.title: Super League
programme.desc: Action from the semi-finals, as the four remaining sides battled it out for places in next weekend's Grand Final

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist continues to explore the secrets behind evolution, this time focusing on the animal kingdom's bizarre hybrids, including the 'pizzly bear', a cross between a polar bear and a grizzly, which is thought to have come into being as a result of global warming. He also covers the killer bee brought into existence due to the transfer of African bees to South America

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made over the years by Days of Wine and Roses actor Jack Lemmon, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by actress Sylvia Syms

programme.title: The Apartment (1960)
programme.desc: A lowly insurance clerk tries to curry favour with his seedy manager by lending him his flat for his extramarital flings. But the clerk soon grows wise to the nastiness of corporate ethics and infidelity when his heartless boss seduces the elevator girl, whom he has always loved from afar. Oscar-winning comedy drama, directed by Billy Wilder and starring Jack Lemmon, Fred MacMurray and Shirley MacLaine

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television appearances made over the years by outspoken actress Shirley MacLaine, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career that led to her being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Coverage from the O2 of the final at the season-ending ATP Finals, which is sure to be a memorable occasion as it is the last time that the star-studded event is to be staged in London. Last year's final at the same venue was a classic with Stefanos Tsitsipas battling past Dominic Thiem in the final-set tie-break of an epic match that lasted over two and a half hours. Presented by Sue Barker, with match commentary and expert analysis by Andrew Castle and Tim Henman

programme.title: The Trials of Oscar Pistorius
programme.desc: A judge is appointed to decide the outcome of the trial rather than a jury, with the task falling to Thokozile Masipa, a highly respected scholar who grew up in the Soweto Township as a child of Apartheid. Despite the dignity with which she attempts to conduct the trial, no-one can stop it from becoming a televised tabloid circus as testimonies provide a shocking insight into Pistorius' character

programme.title: Free State of Jones (2016)
programme.desc: A soldier fighting for the Confederacy during the American Civil War grows disillusioned by the rebel cause and deserts, returning to his Mississippi home. He unites a force of struggling farmers and escaped slaves into a militia, fighting to turn their county into an independent state, free from the rule of both sides. Fact-based drama, starring Matthew McConaughey and Gugu Mbatha-Raw

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter and Adam learn that the Shanti virus is located in Texas after they confront Victoria Pratt, while Hiro continues to pursue his father's murderer. Maya is forced to decide whether she should travel to New York without her brother and Claire deals with recent shocking events. Joanna Cassidy guest stars

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro confronts Peter inside the Primatech facility, forcing him to make a tough decision. Maya is shocked to learn the truth about Sylar, and Micah begs Niki to join him in trying to rescue Monica from her kidnappers. Starring Masi Oka

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac rushes in a mystery patient, whose arrival sets in motion in a deepening medical crisis that could have serious repercussions for her secret relationship with Kian. Keller is shocked when a new member of staff reveals the truth about Sacha's deception, and is tempted to take out his anguish on a vulnerable patient. Cameron basks in the glory of being declared a hero - prompting Nicky to issue him with an ultimatum

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites actress and writer Meera Syal, rapper Guvna B, comedian Dave Gorman and newsreader Babita Sharma to discuss the books that take them back to their youth, the books that transport them to far-flung places and the books that make them feel positive about the world. They also discuss this week's book group pick, Love in Colour by Bolu Babalola, as well as Babita's book The Corner Shop

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with two sets of buyers whose rural aspirations were driven by a spirit of adventure. She first heads to the Welsh Borders for a reunion with a couple who managed to buy their first home - a rural farmhouse with space to accommodate their many hobbies and dogs - in 2017. Nicki then moves on to Devon's south coast to revisit a couple of surfing enthusiasts who, after reassessing their priorities, have successfully built a new life by the sea with their young son

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore uses her salvaging expertise at Witley Recycling Centre to search for three items that she can clean up and reinvent. To help her, she calls on trusty makers Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Duncan McKean. Leigh-Anne takes on two wingback armchairs in need of a makeover, while lighting expert Duncan has some retro lampshades to contend with. Sarah herself sees potential in an extendable dining table

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie drives into the mountains to help with a case that reminds her that being a Mountain Vet is anything but glamorous, as she has to lance a giant boil the size of a watermelon on the leg of a cow. Julie, Castle Vets' head nurse, has seen most things in her long career, but today is a first for her, as a rare wild animal has been brought in, found lying at the side of the road and before assessing what's wrong with it, she must first find out what it is

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the second episode, Mary takes a journey along the Thames in London to discover culinary hidden gems that inspire her to make some deliciously comforting dishes. Her journey starts with the Henley Rowing Club, where she rustles up mixed bean and butternut squash wraps for the team, before heading to The Waterside Inn, the world famous three Michelin Star restaurant run by the Roux family. Later, Mary discovers an oasis floating on the Thames, when she meets an established river community who have turned their barges into gardens teaming with fruit and vegetables

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett in which contestants must answer questions from nine categories, so must choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. The first contestants are a creative writing student, a financial blogger, and a primary school teacher

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Arlo still has not told Lindsey about his feelings for Poppy, and the stress of knowing what he is up to is beginning to take its toll on Billy

programme.title: The Big Painting Challenge
programme.desc: Una Stubbs and Richard Bacon host this art competition in which 10 keen amateurs take on three specially designed challenges each week, showing their skills using a wide range of mediums. The contest begins in Alnwick Castle, Northumberland - which may be familiar to fans of the Harry Potter movies as Hogwarts. Here, the contestants demonstrate their styles for the first time, before a quick-draw challenge and a painting of the castle - after which, painters and expert judges Daphne Todd and Lachlan Goudie decide who will be leaving

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of humans and animals over the course of a year in Alaska, which is by far the biggest US state and still one of the wildest places on Earth with deep forests and vast mountain ranges. The arrival of spring sees Alaska go through a huge transformation as water, light and warmth return, with animals emerging from hibernation and rushing to cash in on the riches of the season. Dougray Scott narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Michael Baggott assess objects brought into the University of Nottingham, while presenter Paul Martin learns about a Nottingham-born Victorian prize-fighter called Bendigo and meets locals who are keeping his memory alive

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David James, Rhys James, Denise Lewis and Isy Suttie go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the fourth couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend, and there are interviews with the remaining pairs

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of Joe Calzaghe, the Beach Boys, Joseph Stalin, and the Gospel of John, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as first-round losers the Ticket Collectors and the Walruses get another chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising Alfonso Valiente, Lionel Richie, Papa, and Antony Kidman

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a layered chocolate and peanut butter cake, and uses liquorice pellets to make a sauce for a Basque burnt cheesecake. She also demonstrates a recipe for roast vegetables with cumin and fennel seeds to accompany spiced bulgur wheat and beetroot sauce

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: A look at the impact of Covid-19 on the Royal Free Hospital's transplant services, which treat some of the trust's most vulnerable patients and were largely unable to continue during the first wave of the pandemic. Four months after lockdown began, the service was finally able to start again and the department performed three months' worth of transplants in five weeks. But as the hospital begins to see infection numbers rise once again, how long can they continue to offer these life-changing operations?

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Russell Kane and Tracy-Ann Oberman to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including the revival of Doctor Who, and Paula Radcliffe winning the London Marathon.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Keeping Britain Fed
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Ade Adepitan gain access to some of Britain's biggest supermarkets and their suppliers to see how they are dealing with the coronavirus outbreak, the most testing time in their history. Sara finds out what goes on in one supermarket over 24 hours, from opening to closing and all through the night, to see how they are coping during the crisis. Elsewhere, Ade finds out how the nations' farms, factories and depots have risen to the challenge to produce and supply our supermarkets with food in these exceptional circumstances

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team descend on Caswell Bay, Swansea, to transform a dilapidated, out-of-use bus shelter into the first, fully adapted surf centre Surfability UK, a charity that provides surfing lessons and experiences for people with disabilities and learning difficulties, and which receives funding from Children in Need. Throughout, Nick presents stories of how children and young people have benefitted from the support of the charity, but the challenge remains tough as this is the team's first build during Covid-19

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham reports live on badger and fox families in the New Forest, while Michaela Strachan catches up with the latest from a seal breeding colony on the Isle of May. Iolo Williams goes in search of a wild parrot-like bird in a secret location in west Wales, and Gillian Burke discovers the surprising wildlife to be found amongst the chimney stacks and industrial ponds of South Yorkshire

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the first heat, four professional chefs from around the country face three challenges to earn a place in this week's quarter final. After the initial test of basic kitchen skills, they must prepare tortellini stuffed with Mushroom Duxelles and served with a sauce of their choice in just 25 minutes. For the final round, they must recreate Marcus Wareing's recipe for devilled butterflied mackerel with herb-flavoured mayo and a cucumber and fennel salad

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Author and architect Francesco da Mosto explores the countryside of Tuscany, Umbria and the Marche. He prepares pasta and picks grapes, before joining actress Maggie Smith to view Florence's art treasures. He also relates the story of St Francis of Assisi and visits a garden once owned by a British woman who fell in love with an Italian aristocrat

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor and intrepid explorer visits Bolivia and Peru, where he meets an Englishwoman restoring a 140-year-old steamer. He then follows the 1,000-mile Urubamba River and sees the Inca ruins of Machu Picchu

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Ambitious detective Katrin 'Kata' Gunnarsdottir investigates when an ex-drug dealer is found murdered in the old harbour area of Reykjavik, and finds that she does not have as much control of her professional life as she had planned. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Detective Arnar Bodvarsson arrives from Oslo to help Kata find a link between the two murders. At the memorial to the latest victim, they catch the prime suspect, who had broken into his father's office. Icelandic crime drama, starring Nina Dogg Filippusdottir. In Icelandic

programme.title: Icelandic Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on a 38-mile journey along Iceland's most famous hiking route. She navigates daunting mountain climbs, red-hot lava fields, freezing river crossings and deadly clouds of sulphuric gas to reach the volcanic crater at the centre of the Eyjafjallajokull glacier

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Swedish journalist and radical left-wing expert Richard Dahlqvist broadcasts live on the internet, denying Red October's involvement in the murder. Saga and Henrik question him, finding what he is says is wide of the truth. They also continue to question Taariq, though finding him unwilling to cooperate form a plan to expose him. The duo search the homeless Danish girls' hiding place, finding evidence that directs them to new lead. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Still at large, Taariq spots the car used to kidnap Margrethe Thormod, and tracks down its owner with the intention of extorting money from him. When another murder at the hospital in Malmo appears to be connected to the two previous ones, Saga finally realises how they are linked and that the killing is probably set to continue. Meanwhile the girls vacate Henrik's house, and while trying to find them Henrik runs into Thomas, the drug dealer. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor and intrepid explorer visits Bolivia and Peru, where he meets an Englishwoman restoring a 140-year-old steamer. He then follows the 1,000-mile Urubamba River and sees the Inca ruins of Machu Picchu

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Author and architect Francesco da Mosto explores the countryside of Tuscany, Umbria and the Marche. He prepares pasta and picks grapes, before joining actress Maggie Smith to view Florence's art treasures. He also relates the story of St Francis of Assisi and visits a garden once owned by a British woman who fell in love with an Italian aristocrat

programme.title: BBC Young Musician
programme.desc: The 2020 final of BBC Young Jazz Musician comes from London's Cadogan Hall. Hosted by saxophonist, composer and broadcaster YolanDa Brown, and filmed in the absence of audience, it features five of the UK's most promising young jazz performers - saxophonists Alex Clarke and Matt Carmichael, bassist Kielan Sheard, pianist Deschanel Gordon and guitarist Ralph Porrett

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: BBC film-maker Natalie Hewit spent three months in Antarctica following the work of the everyday heroes who have been challenged to move a vital polar research station, Halley VI. Built on the Brunt Ice Shelf, it sits far beyond the Antarctic shoreline, but a crevasse in the surrounding ice threatens to send the station adrift

programme.title: Amundsen (2019)
programme.desc: Film drama telling the life story of explorer Roald Amundsen. Inspired by a gift from his father when he was a child, Roald became fascinated by the polar wildernesses. He led the first expedition to the South Pole in 1911 and the first expedition proven to have reached the North Pole, in a dirigible, in 1926. His achievements, however, came at a cost to both his family and his colleagues. Starring Pal Sverre Hagen and Katherine Waterston. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first aired on March 29, with performances by artists including Bizz Nizz, Candy Flip, Jam Tronik, Heart, Jimmy Somerville, the Family Stand, Queen Latifah and De La Soul and Snap

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on April 5, with performances by artists including Jason Donovan, They Might Be Giants, Happy Mondays, Madonna, Queen Latifah and De La Soul, Emma, UB40, Snap and Alannah Myles

programme.title: The Snow Queen
programme.desc: A performance by Scottish Ballet inspired by Hans Christian Andersen's fairytale and celebrating the company's 50th anniversary, recorded at Edinburgh's Festival Theatre

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: BBC film-maker Natalie Hewit spent three months in Antarctica following the work of the everyday heroes who have been challenged to move a vital polar research station, Halley VI. Built on the Brunt Ice Shelf, it sits far beyond the Antarctic shoreline, but a crevasse in the surrounding ice threatens to send the station adrift

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Fred explores the work of the builders responsible for the waterways of Britain. Visiting the Leeds-Liverpool and Bridgewater canals, he explores their industrial history and demonstrates how early pioneers dug the canals and made them watertight, creating some of Britain's finest engineering

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a cozy barn in winter, with the ground blanketed in snow, set against a soft sky

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Dr Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri investigate a painting by 18th-century artist Joseph Wright of Derby, housed at the Derby Museum. The piece suffered an industrial scale restoration in the 1970s, but can the team peel back layers of modern restoration on the mysterious landscape painting stuck in the Derby vaults to reveal another hidden masterpiece?

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Docudrama telling the tale of one of America's most famous unsolved crimes, which involved a mysterious fugitive, a hijacked aeroplane and a daring mid-air escape. The film brings the stories of four possible suspects to life through candid testimony, archive footage and stylised drama. But who is telling the truth, who is lying and, ultimately, who is DB Cooper?

programme.title: The Marvellous World of Roald Dahl
programme.desc: Documentary making use of archived letters, writings and biographical material to tell the story of the life of Roald Dahl, in his own inimitable voice. From his time as a fighter pilot in the Second World War, through to his work as a spy and his encounters with Walt Disney and Theodore Roosevelt, the programme explores how Dahl's personal experiences may have informed the fantastical tales he would eventually publish, including Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and Matilda. Includes contributions by Dahl's last wife Liccy, his daughter Lucy and biographer Donald Sturrock. Long-term collaborator Quentin Blake also provides new animated illustrations based on the events of Dahl's life

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles explores the revolutionary science behind Charles Kingsley's 1863 novel The Water Babies. The broadcaster also looks at the impact the book had on social reform in Victorian England, and how its racist viewpoint has affected the author's reputation

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Actress Samantha Bond examines E Nesbit's Five Children and It, a fantasy story about a group of siblings who discover a creature that can grant wishes. Samantha also meets authors Jacqueline Wilson and Kate Saunders, who have both published updated and re-versioned stories inspired by Nesbit's work

programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo is having a sleepover at Gran Gran's house and wonders why the moon is a funny shape. Gran Gran explains to JoJo why the moon appears to change shape in the night sky, and together they make a moon diary for JoJo to take home to Mummy and Daddy.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady holds a 'knitted treats' sale in the park, but the weather causes a problem

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: In the little Toy House, Pepi Nana shows everybody a book about the rain

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major invents a system so he can hear the other Clangers when they call him for soup

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy cannot decide whether to play with the ball or sing

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Mark Bonnar reads Hugless Douglas, by David Melling

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Liverpool v Leicester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage from day seven at the O2 in London, featuring the opening semi-final of the singles competition. There was an upset in last year's corresponding contest when Stefanos Tsitsipas overcame six-time champion Roger Federer 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite the stars of sci-fi and fantasy to take part in a special edition of the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. This time, contestants Matthew Waterhouse, Louise Jameson, David Graham, Sophia Myles, Jimmy Vee, Lauren Socha, Madeline Smith and Stephanie Beacham take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Lee and Cariad from south Wales, brothers Dion and Kuresh from London, and twins from Liverpool Cathy and Sharon

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the eight remaining couples return to the Strictly Ballroom in a special episode paying tribute to all things Blackpool.
