programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. Unfortunately, he didn't take into account the Queen's tendency to change her mind. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Seattle Seahawks v Arizona Cardinals

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Lifeline

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a quirky terraced house in Birmingham, a flat in a Glasgow high rise block, and a former taxi cab office in north London

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Fulham v Everton (Kick-off 12.00pm). Coverage of the Premier League match from Craven Cottage, where the Cottagers play host to the Toffees. Scott Parker's side have found life difficult on their return to the top flight, picking up just four points from their opening eight games, and will enter this fixture as underdogs despite being the home side. Everton were one of the early season pace-setters but entered the recent international break having lost three games in a row, and will be keen to return to winning ways after such a promising start to the campaign

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins hosts the girls' semi-final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year, with five youngsters hoping to impress judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams scour the Royal Cornwall Showground for items to sell for profit at auction, assisted by experts Caroline Hawley and Ben Cooper. Presented by Eric Knowles

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset, and also visits Dunkeswell Aerodrome

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Witley, Surrey, where she rescues two tip-bound items that she thinks she can turn into a profit for the people getting rid of them

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway returns with more rambling routes around Wales, starting with two easily accessible walks at either end of the country. Accompanied by writer and producer Bedwyr Rees at the seaside village of Moelfre on Anglesey, he follows the coastal path looking at sites of dramatic shipwrecks and hearing about rescues by heroic local lifeboatmen. The second walk picks up this historical theme in the countryside around Dyffryn Gardens in the Vale of Glamorgan. His guide is Will Renwick, who at 22 became the youngest person to cover the whole perimeter of Wales on foot

programme.title: Wales and the History of the World
programme.desc: Eddie Butler reveals the stories behind extraordinary objects that can be found in Welsh museums, beginning with a look at pioneering scientific achievements on land, at sea, and in space

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley are in the Lincolnshire wolds at historic South Ormsby, which has ambitions to be the finest farming estate in the world. They discover the entrepreneurial spirit driving the revival of the local rural economy, including two young women carving out new careers. Elsewhere, Adam Henson has high hopes for an inner-city teenage jockey competing in her first race, while Tom Heap investigates whether it's time to ditch the green belt

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the fourth pair to leave the contest. With music by Billy Ocean, who performs a medley of his hits

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra loses the alethiometer after accepting a lift from Latrom, while Lee Scoresby has an altercation with the strange Dr Haley at the observatory where Grumman used to work and is arrested. In the North, Iorek tells Kaisa that Lyra went through the anomaly, and Mrs Coulter's airship is redirected to Yenisei. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Drama focusing on the romantic reggae genre and to the black youth who found freedom and love in its sound in London house parties, when they were unwelcome in white nightclubs. Drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, who also directed, starring Micheal Ward and Amarah-Jae St Aubyn

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old schoolgirl Muriel Drinkwater was murdered in 1946, brutally assaulted in the woods near her home in Penllergaer, before being shot. Over the decades, although the culprit has never been caught, several key discoveries have been made to advance the investigation. But due in part to the scientific limitations of the day, such discoveries failed to produce major breakthroughs. Here, the team applies modern-day forensic techniques to the available facts. What investigators uncover edges Muriel's killer closer to being identified and provides answers to at least some of the questions that her family has been asking since the day she died

programme.title: Bury: Bringing Football Back
programme.desc: A look at the attempts of a group of locals to bring football back to Bury after the local football club was expelled from the English Football League in August 2019. The film follows Chris Murray and his friends over the course of 12 months, getting behind-the-scenes access to the birth of a new football club and seeing what it really takes to make dreams a reality

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing gold medallist Katherine Grainger, former England striker Dion Dublin and Great Britain 400m greats Christine Ohuruogu and Iwan Thomas join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Wanted Down Under

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: The team investigates what to do after being let down by a wedding photographer. Plus, consumer tips and advice to help prepare for the festive season

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A surprise call from Lauren in New Zealand leaves Peter asking his dad for financial help - can Ian redeem himself? Linda is surprised to find Mick at home nursing a bruised eye, while he tries to cover his displeasure when Max talks about helping her with her new business venture. Kheerat warns Tina about moving in with Gray, claming he is not who people think he is

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. Her arrival causes consternation among the locals - particularly stuffy councillor David Horton, who vows to have her ejected at all costs. Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers co-star

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Journalist Josh Baker investigates the story of former Indiana resident Samantha Sally and her family. After arriving in Syria, her husband became an Islamic State sniper and her young son Matthew was forced to take part in an infamous propaganda video. Sally, who is now in prison, describes her life with the terror group and the part she played in buying sex slaves

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff of Longlox are reunited as they prepare to re-open the salon after lockdown, and while things are going well with Gary, Sammyjo is feeling the pressure to get everything in place for her waiting clients

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The salon opens for its first weekend after lockdown, Gary pays busy Sammyjo a visit, and sad news at home affects Holly's replacement

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live in volcanic hot spots on the ocean bed, areas that reach temperatures hot enough to melt lead and which may hold the secrets behind all life on Earth. Other strange creatures from the depths of the sea include bone-eating worms, fish that walk rather than swim, and hordes of squid. Plus, footage of sharks in a feeding frenzy over the dead body of a whale and the wildlife of the Midnight Zone in the Pacific, an area that never receives any natural light, forcing creatures to rely on their own bioluminescence

programme.desc: The leagues are suspended due to the pandemic, with promotion and relegation are being determined by points per game, leaving Hearts bottom of the Scottish Premiership. Chair Ann Budge is determined to get her club out of crisis as relegation looms, and resorts to legal action against the SPFL

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Journalist Josh Baker investigates the story of former Indiana resident Samantha Sally and her family. After arriving in Syria, her husband became an Islamic State sniper and her young son Matthew was forced to take part in an infamous propaganda video. Sally, who is now in prison, describes her life with the terror group and the part she played in buying sex slaves

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The staff of Longlox are reunited as they prepare to re-open the salon after lockdown, and while things are going well with Gary, Sammyjo is feeling the pressure to get everything in place for her waiting clients

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The salon opens for its first weekend after lockdown, Gary pays busy Sammyjo a visit, and sad news at home affects Holly's replacement

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Richard Osman and Fin Taylor joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Hugh Grant discusses his starring role in psychological thriller The Undoing, while TV cook and food writer Nigella Lawson talks about her new book Cook, Eat, Repeat. Hollywood stars Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey reflect on their involvement in new Disney animation Soul and six-time Oscar-nominated Amy Adams talks about starring in the latest film from Ron Howard, Hillbilly Elegy. Plus, Dua Lipa performs Levitating

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ollie Dabbous and Asma Khan, and special guest Omid Djalili. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Liverpool v Leicester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about Derby's connection to the hot dog

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage from day seven at the O2 in London, featuring the opening semi-final of the singles competition. There was an upset in last year's corresponding contest when Stefanos Tsitsipas overcame six-time champion Roger Federer 6-3, 6-4

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite the stars of sci-fi and fantasy to take part in a special edition of the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. This time, contestants Matthew Waterhouse, Louise Jameson, David Graham, Sophia Myles, Jimmy Vee, Lauren Socha, Madeline Smith and Stephanie Beacham take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are father and daughter Lee and Cariad from south Wales, brothers Dion and Kuresh from London, and twins from Liverpool Cathy and Sharon

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the eight remaining couples return to the Strictly Ballroom in a special episode paying tribute to all things Blackpool. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and guest judge Anton Du Beke, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the sixth episode, couple Melissa and Daniel from Dartford face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. Unfortunately, he didn't take into account the Queen's tendency to change her mind. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Newcastle and Chelsea were both looking to emulate impressive victories in last season's corresponding encounters

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including Seattle Seahawks v Arizona Cardinals

programme.title: Life on the Line (2015)
programme.desc: The head of a power line team is sent in to fix the grid just as a huge storm hits Texas. True-life action thriller, starring John Travolta, Kate Bosworth and Sharon Stone

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Newcastle United v Chelsea at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Martin Kemp presents an appeal on behalf of the Children's Trust, a UK charity helping children and young people recover and rebuild their lives after experiencing a brain injury

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a quirky terraced house in Birmingham, a flat in a Glasgow high rise block, and a former taxi cab office in north London

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Fulham v Everton (Kick-off 12.00pm). Coverage of the Premier League match from Craven Cottage, where the Cottagers play host to the Toffees. Scott Parker's side have found life difficult on their return to the top flight, picking up just four points from their opening eight games, and will enter this fixture as underdogs despite being the home side. Everton were one of the early season pace-setters but entered the recent international break having lost three games in a row, and will be keen to return to winning ways after such a promising start to the campaign

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins hosts the girls' semi-final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year, with five youngsters hoping to impress judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams scour the Royal Cornwall Showground for items to sell for profit at auction, assisted by experts Caroline Hawley and Ben Cooper. Presented by Eric Knowles

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset, and also visits Dunkeswell Aerodrome

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Witley, Surrey, where she rescues two tip-bound items that she thinks she can turn into a profit for the people getting rid of them

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live in volcanic hot spots on the ocean bed, areas that reach temperatures hot enough to melt lead and which may hold the secrets behind all life on Earth. Other strange creatures from the depths of the sea include bone-eating worms, fish that walk rather than swim, and hordes of squid. Plus, footage of sharks in a feeding frenzy over the dead body of a whale and the wildlife of the Midnight Zone in the Pacific, an area that never receives any natural light, forcing creatures to rely on their own bioluminescence

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Joe Crowley are in the Lincolnshire wolds at historic South Ormsby, which has ambitions to be the finest farming estate in the world. They discover the entrepreneurial spirit driving the revival of the local rural economy, including two young women carving out new careers. Elsewhere, Adam Henson has high hopes for an inner-city teenage jockey competing in her first race, while Tom Heap investigates whether it's time to ditch the green belt

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before the judges decide who will be the fourth pair to leave the contest. With music by Billy Ocean, who performs a medley of his hits

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra loses the alethiometer after accepting a lift from Latrom, while Lee Scoresby has an altercation with the strange Dr Haley at the observatory where Grumman used to work and is arrested. In the North, Iorek tells Kaisa that Lyra went through the anomaly, and Mrs Coulter's airship is redirected to Yenisei. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Drama focusing on the romantic reggae genre and to the black youth who found freedom and love in its sound in London house parties, when they were unwelcome in white nightclubs. Drama by Courttia Newland and Steve McQueen, who also directed, starring Micheal Ward and Amarah-Jae St Aubyn

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Sheffield United v West Ham United at Bramall Lane and Leeds United v Arsenal at Elland Road. The Blades were looking to emulate their 1-0 victory over West Ham in last season's corresponding fixture. Leeds and Arsenal met at Emirates Stadium in the third round of last season's FA Cup, when the Gunners won 1-0, but United demonstrated their ability to be competitive against top-flight teams. Plus, a round-up of the rest of the weekend's goals

programme.title: Bury: Bringing Football Back
programme.desc: A look at the attempts of a group of locals to bring football back to Bury after the local football club was expelled from the English Football League in August 2019. The film follows Chris Murray and his friends over the course of 12 months, getting behind-the-scenes access to the birth of a new football club and seeing what it really takes to make dreams a reality

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing gold medallist Katherine Grainger, former England striker Dion Dublin and Great Britain 400m greats Christine Ohuruogu and Iwan Thomas join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Questions are asked when a year after a house fire a second claim comes in, while the data embedded in digital photographs submitted to support stolen items does not add up

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers who toil through the small hours to get the world ready for when the rest of the population awakes. In the first edition, vets perform potentially life-saving surgery on a much-loved horse, and bus cleaners work to have Newcastle's transport ready for commuters

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks are in Leominster, Herefordshire, to help two teams look for items, while presenter Christina Trevanion learns about local photographer Derek Evans

programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph salvages three items from Earlswood Recycling Centre in Surrey, then she and two experts transform four dining chairs, a pair of wardrobe doors and a retro cabinet

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Home Cook Heroes
programme.desc: Suzie continues her mission to get people back into their kitchens by celebrating the humble egg, creating a quiche based on ingredients provided by the Parfitt family from Banbridge. She also talks viewers through egg fried rice on a barbecue, grilled cheese sandwich and an indulgent rich chocolate tart

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A surprise call from Lauren in New Zealand leaves Peter asking his dad for financial help - can Ian redeem himself? Linda is surprised to find Mick at home nursing a bruised eye, while he tries to cover his displeasure when Max talks about helping her with her new business venture. Kheerat warns Tina about moving in with Gray, claming he is not who people think he is

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare classic comfort foods, starting with vegetarian lasagne and an oozy cheesy garlic swirl, followed by a spicy chicken traybake served with pommes Anna. John also whips up sweet and sour prawns and noodles, and Lisa makes her dad's favourite dessert - treacle tart with a special topping

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef begins the Texan leg of his American adventure by visiting a place with more than a hint of Mexican spice, as San Antonio was once a part of Mexico. Amidst the wonders of the city, James tries fresh churros and a traditional barbecoa - Mexican barbecue

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of three races from Ascot, including the 2.05 Grade 2 Chase and 2.40 Ascot Hurdle. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep

programme.title: Catchphrase: Catchiest Moments

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of three races from Ascot, including the 2.05 Grade 2 Chase and 2.40 Ascot Hurdle. programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK

programme.desc: Francine's attraction to Stan wanes after he becomes addicted to slot-car racing, and Roger becomes an air-stewardess in an attempt to secure an audience for his comedy routines

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Junior is given a makeover, and decides to ask a girl at school to be his prom date - but she mistakes him for a woman. Meanwhile, Cleveland's knowledge of his favourite snack food is put to the test. With the guest voice of Rashida Jones

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland is sent to install cable TV for rapper Kenny West (voiced by real-life hip-hop star Kanye West), but after spotting all the star's awards, decides to confront him about not sharing the credit for a song they wrote together. Meanwhile, Cleveland Jr is rejected by the school's marching band, so he starts up a polka group

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts as Max George, Nigel Ng, Amelia Lily, Georgia Steel, Helen Bauer and Will Manning try to win £2,500 and snail and garlic flavour crisps for members of the public

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson guides viewers through some more weird and wonderful Guinness World Records, including the fastest time to pull an aircraft, the most watermelons punched in one minute and the longest time to sing suspended upside down

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Hollywood star Matthew McConaughey talks about his book Greenlights. Plus, country newcomer Gabby Barrett performs her song Hope.

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate attends his sentencing hearing, Matty is concerned about Moira, and Chas dishes out some home truths

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny and Jenny find Peter in the bar, barely conscious, and Carla is alarmed when he is taken to hospital. Meanwhile, Todd's presence shocks an unsuspecting Summer, and Tracy confides her worries to Emma

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam and Daniel are horrified to find Peter in Victoria Garden clutching an unopened bottle of whisky. Sean gets the measure of Todd, and Tracy and Emma share their memory box idea with Amy

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy and Jonah steel themselves for a barrage of jokes and snide comments about their sex video as they return from a four-month suspension just in time for the back-to-school sale madness. However, they receive different treatment from their co-workers. Elsewhere, Jeff attempts to woo back Mateo. Comedy, starring America Ferrera

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Jonah encourages Amy to have a small baby shower in order to stock up on much-needed baby supplies, but things quickly get out of hand. Meanwhile, Mateo art directs the photo shoot for Dina's Christmas card

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: The celebrities continue to battle it out in the race to be crowned King or Queen of the castle. As ever, Ant and Dec will be looking back on the past 24 hours in camp, and overseeing the next stage of events in Wales

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking contestants Bob Whiteman, Adei Bundy and Robert Cooper to 'say what you see' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues to win £50,000 for charity

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate attends his sentencing hearing, Matty is concerned about Moira, and Chas dishes out some home truths

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny and Jenny find Peter in the bar, barely conscious, and Carla is alarmed when he is taken to hospital. Meanwhile, Todd's presence shocks an unsuspecting Summer, and Tracy confides her worries to Emma

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam and Daniel are horrified to find Peter in Victoria Garden clutching an unopened bottle of whisky. Sean gets the measure of Todd, and Tracy and Emma share their memory box idea with Amy

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Ellen talks to big-screen stars Sarah Paulson and Kiera Allen, who discuss starring in new thriller Run

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in Manchester, where they try to win the affections of fashion stylist Courtney by each buying him an outfit

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another bunch of domestic mishaps, captured in video form by viewers. This time, a Brexit victory dance ends in disaster, a runaway car demolishes a wall, and a dog eats Lady Gaga

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: The celebrities continue to battle it out in the race to be crowned King or Queen of the castle. As ever, Ant and Dec will be looking back on the past 24 hours in camp, and overseeing the next stage of events in Wales

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity - The Daily Drop
programme.desc: Joining presenter Vick Hope are a stellar panel of ex-campmates and celebrity superfans who will delve, debate and dissect all of the daily action from the camp

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates another selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile phone footage. Featuring giddy grandmas, wedding nightmares and more below-the-belt mishaps

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another round of viewer-submitted comedy clips, as captured via smartphone, drone and CCTV. This time, a hoverboard destroys an office, a man falls through a casino ceiling, and an ostrich takes a shine to a young child's mobile phone

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces another selection of viewer-submitted mishaps, captured in video form via smartphones, drones and other home-recording equipment. This time, a drone is destroyed by an errant baseball, a seal refuses to join in on a selfie, and a falling tree traps what seems to be a familiar face in the toilet

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Return of the award-winning American comedy. In the first episode, Walden decides to reprioritise his life following a health scare on Halloween. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Walden decide to get married so Walden can adopt a child. However, Alan has second thoughts when Walden makes him sign a pre-nuptual agreement. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is asked by Joe to be godfather to his daughter, but he commits to his mistaken interpretation of the role so much that he instead becomes a real gangster

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine and Hayley take a break from their husbands, while Stan and Jeff pretend to be frontiersmen

programme.title: Crossing Swords
programme.desc: Patrick lands a coveted squire's position at the royal castle, but his dream job becomes a nightmare. Animated fantasy comedy, with the voice of Nicholas Hoult

programme.title: Crossing Swords
programme.desc: Patrick is enlisted to carry out an execution aimed at improving King Merriman's sagging poll numbers. Animated fantasy comedy, with the voice of Nicholas Hoult

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan buys the rights to a Broadway show after inheriting money from an uncle

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and the gang are forced to join the United States Coast Guard after being caught pretending to be military veterans

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian convinces Stewie to teach him how to invest in the stock market, while Quagmire, Cleveland, and Joe look after the Drunken Clam while Jerome is away

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is crushed when he realises his loved ones have forgotten about Father's Day, so he gives them a chance to make it up to him by celebrating belatedly. When he is less than thrilled with the results, he uses the CIA's Memory-Neutraliser to Groundhog Day the family, erasing their memories of the last 24 hours and forcing them to relive the holiday until it is perfect

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, Big Narstie and Babatunde Aleshe go up against Lady Leshurr, Fern Brady, M Huncho and Nafe Smallz. They go head to head in rap battles, dance-offs and freestyling challenges. Jordan Stephens hosts, with help from DJ Shortee Blitz on the decks

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: An exploration of the Brown-Tubbs family's various incarnations around the world. Cleveland is unhappy with Junior's choice of birthday celebration in Mexico and must choose a successor in a mob-style parody set in Italy. Meanwhile, in Japan, he builds a robot version of himself to work late at the office

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna wants to renew her marriage vows with Cleveland, but decides to sign them up for relationship counselling when he ruins the ceremony. Meanwhile, Junior's refrigerator breaks down, so Rallo refurbishes it as a hotel-style mini-bar to generate extra income from his brother

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden's former business partner Billy (guest star Patton Oswalt) turns up at the beach house to present his old colleague with a new money-making scheme. However, the pair's frosty relationship threatens to get in the way of success. Meanwhile, a lonely Alan goes to visit Berta. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Conchata Ferrell

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: With Zoey out of town, Walden ends up hanging out with Jake and Eldridge, who enlist him to drive them to a high-school party. Meanwhile, Alan and Lyndsey decide to introduce their mothers to each other

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is delighted when Mike announces his intention to marry into wealth - until a close link between the two families is uncovered

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Peter faces danger following a row with Carla, Leanne tries to persuade Steve to change his mind, and Craig gains an insight into Ray's grand plan.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Carry On Henry (1971)
programme.desc: King Henry VIII plots to rid himself of his new French wife when her craving for garlic becomes too much to cope with. However, he cannot afford to anger the king of France, and tries to make it look as if his queen has had an affair. Comedy, starring Sid James, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Terry Scott, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A man's apparent suicide in an armoured car shocks the village of Morton Fendle, but things take a more sinister turn upon the discovery he was not alone when he died. Top of Barnaby and Jones's list of suspects is the victim's missing girlfriend - whose mysterious hold over the war-blighted residents could provide the key to the mystery. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A mischievous trickster finally gets his comeuppance when his body is found floating below the weir in Midsomer Deverell. The extent of his double life starts to emerge when three family friends and a long-lost niece produce wills claiming his fortune, and his collection of erotic photographs from the 1960s gives Barnaby and Jones the key to the tangled relationships and feuds blighting the family

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a family glassware business collapses following the death of one of its partners, Barnaby investigates allegations of financial irregularities, love triangles and a string of murders. Lapsed Freemason Jones uses his inside knowledge to explore the ceremonial aspects of two killings, but it is Cully's production of Hamlet that gives Tom the key to the mystery. John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As Morse sees in 1970 at a Venetian opera, Thursday and the team are faced with a grisly murder on an Oxford towpath. Back in Oxford, Morse meets a kindred spirit, while a ground-breaking college project becomes a fatal battle of the sexes. Shaun Evans stars as the cerebral detective, in an episode he also directed, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser and Sean Rigby

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: George refuses to apologise for his marriage to Amelia and his father sends him a letter, cutting him off from the family and denying him his inheritance. All the friends set sail for Belgium, where Becky makes use of every opportunity to advance in society, laying on the charm with the great and the good. Even George is captivated by Becky's new glamour, as Amelia notices with mounting dread. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manoeuvres her way to the top of Georgian high society. Starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid forgets an important party - unfortunately the occasion is his silver wedding anniversary. Sid James stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's tries to join the pop scene make him a laughing stock and he realises the generation gap is wider than ever

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Peter faces danger following a row with Carla, Leanne tries to persuade Steve to change his mind, and Craig gains an insight into Ray's grand plan.

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Dawn makes Jamie an offer, Vinny tells Mandy he is moving out, and Belle's mental health continues to spiral

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Dr Alan Delaney is killed by a hit-and-run driver while in charge of his partner's new car, Barnaby and Jones discover the dead man had once been accused by Dr James Kirkwood of stealing a large sum of money from their Midsomer Market practice. Further investigations to uncover the whereabouts of the missing money lead the detectives to believe that Kirkwood may have been the real target. Guest starring Stephanie Cole (Doc Martin), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Respected surgeon Dan Marsden is shot dead days before his retirement and his teenage daughters Mira and Karen are abducted. Joined by a new addition to the ranks, Vera investigates a mysterious parcel and a connection to the Iraq War as she races against time to catch the killer and save the girls. However, the case soon takes a violent and unexpected turn, striking at the heart of the team. Guest starring Saskia Reeves (Luther) and Dean Andrews (Ashes to Ashes), with Brenda Blethyn, David Leon and Gareth Farr

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: Craig Petrie becomes a suspect in both murders, and Blake assists in questioning him. However, he quickly turns the tables on the police and casts doubt on Leighton's innocence. Annie investigates his claims further, and her loyalty to Leighton is shaken when she discovers his alibi is a lie. Smilie tries to comfort Jonjo's father, but is rejected

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones sets out to explore eight very disparate kinds of quintessentially British terrain, investigating how they have dictated different ways of living, working and playing - both in the past and as they are now - across the entire country, while also unearthing quirky and funny facts en route. He begins by heading to the South Downs, which are situated in Hampshire and East and West Sussex, taking to the air for a bird's eye view and attempting to get to know one of the region's woolliest inhabitants

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Dawn makes Jamie an offer, Vinny tells Mandy he is moving out, and Belle's mental health continues to spiral

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Angie turns up to visit Curly and catch up with all the gossip. Tricia swears to Vera that Brad is Terry's son. Fiona is upset to hear Alan has kept her away from work parties

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin finds Chris sleeping rough in the garage. Don blames Mike for Josie leaving him and marrying someone else. Angie encourages Des to sell up and go off on his boat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara and Kim go shopping for a wedding dress. Chris and Zoe go to Leeds to find Daniels, who has a shock revelation for them regarding the lady in the lake's death

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris cannot believe Zoe has taken Daniels at his word. Tara asks Steve why he is hiding his family from Kim. Andy and Robert set up Jack and Sarah for a night out

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker and Miller struggle to decide whether an ex-Army major is guilty of murder after shooting a youth who broke into his home, while Carol tries to get through to a pregnant teenager. Rosie learns the garage is in financial trouble - unaware Peggy and a salesman with big ideas (impressionist Jon Culshaw) have already dreamed up a plan that could save it. Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop befriends a pilot he rescues from his crashed plane, but suspects he is being drawn into a smuggling racket when the family is invited to a celebratory champagne night on the town. Drama, starring David Jason

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Hugh organises a cricket match between Greek and English teams to bring everyone together. Countess Mavrodaki offers to umpire - but warns Margo that contests between the two nations are never friendly. Spiro advises Louisa that she has terrible taste in men, while Larry finds that Vasilia wants their relationship to progress faster than expected

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A beauty queen is targeted by mysterious attackers ahead of the Miss Yorkshire contest, which attracts more than the usual share of attention when busloads of female protesters turn up to picket the event. Ordered to boost his arrest record, Younger finds a novel way of raising the village's crime rate, while Phil spies an opportunity to get closer to Gina. Steven Blakeley and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly flirts with Chris, Marlon is in a panic when the TV crew arrives, and Zak and Butch's plans to interfere with the programme being filmed do not go to plan

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric blames Marlon for all the chaos at the tearooms. Sarah kicks Biff and Marlon out of the cottage when she discovers them having a party. Zak panics ahead of his wedding day

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is disgusted by the way Andy appears to be hungover all the time. Ashley is thrilled with having Maxine as a girlfriend. Mike sacks his whole workforce

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona tells Alan she needs to be sure before she agrees to marry him. Don lets himself into Mike's factory at night, with a can of petrol

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A photographer is murdered shortly after exhibiting a compromising picture of Barnaby - who becomes a suspect in the ensuing investigation. Joyce concludes that someone is trying to frame him, and is stunned when he admits to a previous relationship with the murdered man's wife. Jones is forced to work with the irritating Martin Spellman to solve the case. Guest starring Liza Goddard, Adrian Scarborough and Richard Lintern

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa have their first fertility consultation with Dr Emma Ryder. Meanwhile, a case of vandalism at a local surf school is reported and PC Joe Penhale investigates. Elsewhere, Dr Edward Mullen returns to the surgery, and Bert is feeling the pressure from Caitlin. Starring Martin Clunes, Lucy Russell and Conleth Hill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Reeling from his family tragedy, Will seeks distraction in Geordie's world where a curious case unfolds concerning the murder of a Teddy Boy at a dance hall. The dead boy's pockets reveal a library card from Esme's school, and even more disturbing - a picture of Esme herself. Geordie makes a beeline for the school and grills his frightened daughter. She pleads ignorance, but Will knows something is up when he spots her hiding a love letter. Away from her life at the vicarage, Mrs Chapman feels lost, while Cathy is left shaken by another encounter at work. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Dr Alan Delaney is killed by a hit-and-run driver while in charge of his partner's new car, Barnaby and Jones discover the dead man had once been accused by Dr James Kirkwood of stealing a large sum of money from their Midsomer Market practice. Further investigations to uncover the whereabouts of the missing money lead the detectives to believe that Kirkwood may have been the real target. Guest starring Stephanie Cole (Doc Martin), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Respected surgeon Dan Marsden is shot dead days before his retirement and his teenage daughters Mira and Karen are abducted. Joined by a new addition to the ranks, Vera investigates a mysterious parcel and a connection to the Iraq War as she races against time to catch the killer and save the girls. However, the case soon takes a violent and unexpected turn, striking at the heart of the team. Guest starring Saskia Reeves (Luther) and Dean Andrews (Ashes to Ashes), with Brenda Blethyn, David Leon and Gareth Farr

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: Craig Petrie becomes a suspect in both murders, and Blake assists in questioning him. However, he quickly turns the tables on the police and casts doubt on Leighton's innocence. Annie investigates his claims further, and her loyalty to Leighton is shaken when she discovers his alibi is a lie. Smilie tries to comfort Jonjo's father, but is rejected

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones sets out to explore eight very disparate kinds of quintessentially British terrain, investigating how they have dictated different ways of living, working and playing - both in the past and as they are now - across the entire country, while also unearthing quirky and funny facts en route. He begins by heading to the South Downs, which are situated in Hampshire and East and West Sussex, taking to the air for a bird's eye view and attempting to get to know one of the region's woolliest inhabitants

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Dawn makes Jamie an offer, Vinny tells Mandy he is moving out, and Belle's mental health continues to spiral

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Dawn makes Jamie an offer, Vinny tells Mandy he is moving out, and Belle's mental health continues to spiral

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Angie turns up to visit Curly and catch up with all the gossip. Tricia swears to Vera that Brad is Terry's son. Fiona is upset to hear Alan has kept her away from work parties

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin finds Chris sleeping rough in the garage. Don blames Mike for Josie leaving him and marrying someone else. Angie encourages Des to sell up and go off on his boat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara and Kim go shopping for a wedding dress. Chris and Zoe go to Leeds to find Daniels, who has a shock revelation for them regarding the lady in the lake's death

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris cannot believe Zoe has taken Daniels at his word. Tara asks Steve why he is hiding his family from Kim. Andy and Robert set up Jack and Sarah for a night out

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker and Miller struggle to decide whether an ex-Army major is guilty of murder after shooting a youth who broke into his home, while Carol tries to get through to a pregnant teenager. Rosie learns the garage is in financial trouble - unaware Peggy and a salesman with big ideas (impressionist Jon Culshaw) have already dreamed up a plan that could save it. Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop befriends a pilot he rescues from his crashed plane, but suspects he is being drawn into a smuggling racket when the family is invited to a celebratory champagne night on the town. Drama, starring David Jason

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Hugh organises a cricket match between Greek and English teams to bring everyone together. Countess Mavrodaki offers to umpire - but warns Margo that contests between the two nations are never friendly. Spiro advises Louisa that she has terrible taste in men, while Larry finds that Vasilia wants their relationship to progress faster than expected

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A beauty queen is targeted by mysterious attackers ahead of the Miss Yorkshire contest, which attracts more than the usual share of attention when busloads of female protesters turn up to picket the event. Ordered to boost his arrest record, Younger finds a novel way of raising the village's crime rate, while Phil spies an opportunity to get closer to Gina. Steven Blakeley and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly flirts with Chris, Marlon is in a panic when the TV crew arrives, and Zak and Butch's plans to interfere with the programme being filmed do not go to plan

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric blames Marlon for all the chaos at the tearooms. Sarah kicks Biff and Marlon out of the cottage when she discovers them having a party. Zak panics ahead of his wedding day

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is disgusted by the way Andy appears to be hungover all the time. Ashley is thrilled with having Maxine as a girlfriend. Mike sacks his whole workforce

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona tells Alan she needs to be sure before she agrees to marry him. Don lets himself into Mike's factory at night, with a can of petrol

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A photographer is murdered shortly after exhibiting a compromising picture of Barnaby - who becomes a suspect in the ensuing investigation. Joyce concludes that someone is trying to frame him, and is stunned when he admits to a previous relationship with the murdered man's wife. Jones is forced to work with the irritating Martin Spellman to solve the case. Guest starring Liza Goddard, Adrian Scarborough and Richard Lintern

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa have their first fertility consultation with Dr Emma Ryder. Meanwhile, a case of vandalism at a local surf school is reported and PC Joe Penhale investigates. Elsewhere, Dr Edward Mullen returns to the surgery, and Bert is feeling the pressure from Caitlin. Starring Martin Clunes, Lucy Russell and Conleth Hill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Reeling from his family tragedy, Will seeks distraction in Geordie's world where a curious case unfolds concerning the murder of a Teddy Boy at a dance hall. The dead boy's pockets reveal a library card from Esme's school, and even more disturbing - a picture of Esme herself. Geordie makes a beeline for the school and grills his frightened daughter. She pleads ignorance, but Will knows something is up when he spots her hiding a love letter. Away from her life at the vicarage, Mrs Chapman feels lost, while Cathy is left shaken by another encounter at work. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh

programme.desc: Jonathan and Drew Scott help Gus and Denise, whose three-bedroom home is bursting at the seams and means they are desperate to find a new property that suits their growing needs

programme.title: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Drew helps a career couple and their four kids find a bigger space that suits their family and Jonathan renovates their old home with special attention given to the master bedroom

programme.title: Dinner Date

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Cheshire
programme.desc: Seema and Nicole question another housewife's integrity when a series of anonymous direct messages provide an insight into their true feelings for their friends

programme.title: Totally Unbelievable
programme.desc: High-prices lawyers, tell-all stories in the media - when celebrity couples decide to 'consciously uncouple', fireworks are certain to follow.

programme.title: Botched
programme.desc: Ravaged by a flesh-eating disease, a young woman seeks Terry Dubrow and Paul Nassif help to save what is left of her breasts. Plus, visits by a Southern woman who wants to lose her witch-like nose and a world-famous drag queen who wants help for some silicone slip-ups

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Georgia organises a trip to Sardinia, but Tommy struggles to relax despite the help of an old friend. Back in Essex, Courtney tries to move on from Myles

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Robert asks if he can borrow the suitcase sitting on the stairway landing, Ray explains he and Debra have been locked in a two-week battle of wills over who is responsible for putting the item away. Ray Romano and Brad Garrett star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Marie shame Ray into giving Robert a second bachelor party to make up for his disappointing first attempt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Robert and Amy prepare to tie the knot in a classic Barone family wedding ceremony - but the groom becomes suspicious when his future brother-in-law Peter tries to make peace with both him and Ray. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Attractive and popular psychologist Dr Honey Snow dates Frasier, who is so flattered that he agrees to write the introduction to her latest book - a decision that later causes him a king-size crisis of conscience. Guest starring Shannon Tweed

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Cheers owner Sam Malone turns up in Seattle to ask for advice on whether to go through with his wedding - but Frasier recognises his old friend's fiancee as a woman he slept with three months previously. Comedy, guest starring Ted Danson and Tea Leoni

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink casually wanders into Daphne's bedroom, making her furious with him. He apologises, but inadvertently does it again and she threatens to leave, forcing him to shower her with gifts to make her stay. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After spending the holidays apart, the gang get together to hear about Sheldon's and Amy's trip to his mother's, Leonard and Penny recall their Christmas tree adventure, and Howard and Bernadette open up about their first days as parents

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon stumbles upon a major scientific breakthrough, causing him to feel like a fraud, while Penny steps up her game with Leonard after seeing Howard make a romantic gesture to Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tests a newly invented device after hearing that it can help him to read people's emotions. Leonard isn't happy when he finds out that Penny's brother is coming to town so that she can help him to find a job. Raj gathers his ex-girlfriends together so that they can give him some dating advice

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Howard attempt to distract Sheldon with a trip to a historic railway when he slows the progress of their work on the Air Force guidance system. Raj and Stuart look after baby Halley, while Penny and Amy take Bernadette out

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Tension between Springfield Elementary's fourth and fifth graders spills over during a dodgeball game, leading Bart to arrange a fight with a fellow pupil. Marge gets back in touch with three friends she fell out with years ago, but she soon realises why they went their separate ways

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe achieves massive success when he turns the tavern into a gay bar, but keeps his hetrosexuality a secret to remain in his new clientele's favour. However, the decision backfires when the pub's new regulars encourage him to run for office and become Springfield's first openly gay councilman. Meanwhile, Principal Skinner falls for a new music teacher. Guest starring Kristen Wiig (Bridesmaids) and Alyson Hannigan (How I Met Your Mother)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes obsessed with a father-son relationship in a 1980s sitcom, using it as a source of inspiration to deal with Bart's hankering for a must-have mini-bike. However, when the youngster starts trading nuclear secrets with the Chinese government, Homer must find a new way to bring his son into line

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge notices grey hairs creeping through her blue dye and decides to embrace the shade like other middle-aged women. Homer becomes Moe's official 'wing man' in a bid to boost his friend's ailing love life

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Bart create a cartoon that is bought by a major animation studio and turned into a Golden Globe-nominated short. The pair are stunned to learn they have won the award, but Bart's joy is short-lived when Homer steals all the glory. Guest starring Ricky Gervais, Russell Brand, Nick Park, JB Smoove and Halle Berry

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Former tattoo artist Ben Cullen feels the pressure as he is asked to make a caricature of a cake-maker for a 50th birthday party. Nastassja tackles an unconventional fifth wedding anniversary cake for a couple who want a work of art, rather than a traditional cake, hand-decorated with hundreds of edible paper strips

programme.title: World Chase Tag - USA
programme.desc: Highlights from Atlanta, Georgia, as 16 teams from around America competed for a cash prize

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). Lee McKenzie presents all the action from the Autumn Nations Cup Group A encounter at Twickenham. England prevailed 24-12 when the sides met here in the Six Nations back in February with the help of tries by George Ford, Elliot Daly and Luke Cowan-Dickie. With analysis by Rory Best and Chris Ashton, commentary from Nolli Waterman and Jerry Flannery, and pitchside reports by Ed Jackson

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Elvis fan Sandra and her husband Desmond seek help in their search for their dream holiday home near Mar Menor's stunning beaches on Spain's Costa Calida. They would ideally like a property with three bedrooms for when their family come to visit. Danni Menzies has five options for their £150,000 budget, but whether the couple will be impressed enough to put in an offer remains to be seen

programme.title: Surviving the Stone Age
programme.desc: The tribe's newly appointed lead hunter makes his first kill, a large male deer, after several days spent stalking the forest. The much-needed meat is soon being preserved to keep the tribe going, while the skin and bones are used to make more tools. Some downtime provides the experts with the opportunity to make rock-face art

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts visits Portsmouth to investigate the history of the Royal Navy, uncovering some uncomfortable truths behind the myth surrounding Lord Horatio Nelson and getting first-hand experience of what life would have been like in the heat of battle at Trafalgar. She also visits an archaeological dig on Burrow Island and a lantern show at the New Theatre Royal, and discovers the brutal punishments doled out for even minor offences by the Victorian penal system

programme.title: The Martian (2015)
programme.desc: A manned mission to Mars is abruptly abandoned, with one crew member left for dead. However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. Ridley Scott's sci-fi drama based on the novel by Andy Weir, starring Matt Damon, Jessica Chastain, Kristen Wiig and Chiwetel Ejiofor

programme.title: X-Men: The Last Stand (2006)
programme.desc: The mutant heroes return to face a battle on three fronts. Arch-enemy Magneto is back with an army of followers, while a corporation has created a drug that permanently robs mutants of their powers. Meanwhile, their former ally Jean Grey has returned from the dead as the destructive and seemingly unstoppable Phoenix. Third instalment of the superhero saga, starring Hugh Jackman, Ian McKellen, Famke Janssen, Halle Berry and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Springfield is the setting for parodies of Mad Men, Michael Bay's Transformers and It's the Great Pumpkin, Charlie Brown in this Halloween-themed episode. Homer's vote on election day is misread by a renegade machine, Lisa's Malibu Stacy car turns into a robot determined to start a war, and a giant pumpkin seeks revenge

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Double-act Cheech and Chong split during their Springfield reunion show, leaving Homer to step into the breach and help out one of his comedy heroes. The new line-up is so successful that Cheech decides to partner Homer full-time, leaving Chong to recruit Principal Skinner as the other half of his new act

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Another trio of spirited Halloween tales. Board games from bygone eras wage war against Springfield, Homer and Marge spend their second honeymoon adrift in the Pacific Ocean, and a mysterious fanged student woos Lisa. With the guest voices of Daniel Radcliffe and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Omnibus. Ste offers Jordan some home truths, and Mitchell encourages Martine to go on a date, renewing Toby's resentment towards his brother

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sushi Ko, a Japanese restaurant in California that is losing up to $20,000 a month. Gordon finds owner and chef Akira lacking in confidence and self-esteem as the strain of an empty business is beginning to take its toll on his relationship with this family

programme.title: Fifteen to One
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig returns with the general knowledge quiz in which 15 contestants test themselves against each other to gain a place in the end-of-series final

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra complains she and Ray do not have any fun together - and persuades him to let her join him in his game of golf. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star

programme.desc: Amy is furious when she learns that Sheldon has shared intimate details of their relationship with his colleagues at the university, prompting their first fight since moving in together. Raj considers trying to live without financial support from his father, who thinks he is spoilt

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Cut off from his father's fortune, Raj struggles to balance his books, so decides to appoint Sheldon to organise his finances and get him out of debt. Penny considers joining the guys at Comic-Con because she thinks Leonard wants her to come along

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
