programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa notices how a patch of wild flowers has a calming effect on two aggressive scorpions, and discovers it has a similar impact on Springfield's human population. A pharmaceutical company soon gets involved, leaving Bart to spot a potential money-making scheme

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa becomes an apprentice to famous magician the Great Raymondo, but trouble brews when she is persuaded to reveal the secret behind the her master's most famous trick. Meanwhile, Marge develops an obsession with peach-based desserts after befriending a folk singer. Guest starring Martin Landau (Ed Wood) and Jack McBrayer (30 Rock)

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer are joined by Sandi Toksvig, Richard Osman, Chris and Rosie Ramsey, Tim Minchin, Josh Groban and Omid Djalili. Plus there is music by Yungblud

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer are invited on a weekend away at the Jersey Shore to smooth over their differences with Fat Tony and Selma, who have married after a whirlwind romance. Bart discovers a talent for detecting the location of truffles, which Lisa jumps at the chance to nurture

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer discovers a talent for styling hair and opens a salon that becomes very popular, but begins to feel his gift is a curse as he must listen to people's problems and gossip. Meanwhile, Milhouse decides to live each day to the fullest and professes his love for Lisa - only to have his heart broken

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa gets her hands on a collection of keys to every door in Springfield, and soon uncovers a hidden classroom beneath the school, telling Principal Skinner about her discovery. He confiscates the key from her, but she remains determined to investigate the mysterious room

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present this year's semi-final, the last four bakers facing three patisserie challenges to decide who will be going through to next week's final. The signature round features plenty of alcohol, while the technical challenge sets the bakers the task of making pastry horns. Finally, for the Showstopper, they must use all the precision they can muster to construct a cake made entirely from smaller cakes

programme.title: Flushed Away (2006)
programme.desc: A pampered pet rodent is flushed down a toilet and ends up in a vast underground rat city in the sewers of London. He is befriended by a quick-witted rat who offers to help him find his way home, but first the hapless pair have to escape the clutches of an evil toad bent on ridding the rodent metropolis of its residents. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Kate Winslet, Ian McKellen, Shane Richie, Jean Reno and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda 2 (2011)
programme.desc: Martial arts master panda Po continues to hone his skills when an evil peacock, who was banished from China for trying to build a deadly new weapon, returns to cause more chaos. It falls to Po to defeat him, but he suffers strange flashbacks revealing a connection between his past and the sinister bird. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jack Black and Gary Oldman

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp creates quick and easy crafts for the festive season. She is joined by Phil Spencer, who gets the party started with some cocktail magic. Kirstie's sister, florist Sofie, helps with some festive florals that can be scaled up or down for any size of gathering. And in the kitchen, baker Nancy Birtwhistle has a raft of recipes, tips and tricks for all things foodie and festive

programme.title: Call Me by Your Name (2017)

The chef tries to teach her the basics of cooking and venue management, but trouble brews when her staff threaten to walk out on relaunch night

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.desc: A prank against Frasier nearly backfires as he prepares to leave Lilith for Cheers' new bar manager Rebecca. Kirstie Alley and Kelsey Grammer star

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie tries to gain the upper hand in her relationship with new daughter-in-law Amy, but Debra is determined the latest Barone wife will not endure the kind of interference she has had to put up with

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Michael stays home from school for two days in a row, causing Ray to wonder whether he is trying to avoid something. Determined to discover the truth, he deprives the youngster of television, comics and video games to sweat the answer out of him through sheer boredom

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray grow irritated by Robert and Amy's over-the-top displays of affection - especially when the pair start giving them relationship advice. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star

programme.desc: The Crane brothers are both invited to join a prestigious gentlemen's club - but unfortunately there is only a single vacancy available, meaning one of them is destined for disappointment. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

In an effort to thwart any assassination attempt he hires a female bodyguard to protect him at an awards ceremony. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.desc: John Carona, the founder and CEO of property management company Associa, goes undercover in his own organisation to see first-hand how his employees - including a maintenance supervisor and technician, a concierge and a home inspector - are responding to recent changes at the firm

In order to save the struggling Italian restaurant from disaster, he must persuade harassed owner Julie to co-operate with the head chef, and work his magic on a menu that relies heavily on a microwave

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe helps Homer and Marge work on their marriage, with the couple not knowing that an elderly man has a bet with Mr Burns on whether he will break them apart

programme.desc: Mitchell's loved ones panic when he is nowhere to be found. Meanwhile, Kurt returns from his trip to France with his plan to double-cross his friends in full swing

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team investigates the secrets behind foods that are claimed to work wonders for people's health, with Jimmy Doherty examining whether prunes really are good for backed-up bowels. Kate Quilton learns that caffeine can have unexpected benefits when it comes to losing weight, while Matt Tebbutt looks at reports that too much kale could actually be a bad thing

programme.title: Is Covid Racist?
programme.desc: A&E medic Dr Ronx sets out to understand why so many NHS colleagues who died from coronavirus came from Black, Asian and minority ethnic groups. Featuring stories of frontline workers who have lost their lives, and a striking visual tribute in memory of many more, Dr Ronx asks what is being done to protect frontline BAME workers

programme.title: America's Sex Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following a team of female police officers trying to pull 15-year-old Nadia away from selling sex on the streets of Phoenix, Arizona, a life she has been lured into. Film-maker Jezza Neumann gets to know not only Nadia, and another victim called Kat, but also goes home with the officers, meeting their kids, and learning what drives them to go to such lengths to reach out to girls on the streets, who often see them as the enemy

programme.title: Educating Greater Manchester
programme.desc: The pressure is on at Harrop Fold as GCSE exams get underway, and the school finds itself hitting the headlines when four members of staff are suspended over allegations of removing disadvantaged and struggling pupils from the school roll to improve exam results. The new heads girl launches an online campaign against uniform policy that lands her in trouble. Mr Walker is concerned that year 10 pupil Morgan may not be able to catch up with Spanish in time for her exams, and Mr Povey intervenes to find out the underlying cause of her disruptive behaviour

programme.title: Damilola: The Boy Next Door
programme.desc: A poignant and reflective documentary marking the 20th anniversary of 10-year-old Damilola Taylor's violent murder on a North Peckham Estate in south London, presented by the radio presenter, writer and former resident of the estate Yinka Bokinni, who was a childhood friend of Damilola. Throughout the film, Yinka attempts to reconcile the two versions of her past and try to understand how the warm and loving community she remembers so fondly from her childhood was presented in the media as a notorious, crime-ridden estate that was somehow to blame for the tragic loss of a young life

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand presents the companion show to the baking contest, inviting guests Joe Wilkinson, Jordan Banjo and Prue Leith to reflect on Patisserie Week. Regular contributor Tom Allen waves his whisk at the socially distanced home bakers and their tasty treats, while Jo introduces unseen footage and an exclusive interview with the baker leaving the competition. The host also unearths the delights and the frights being baked in viewers' kitchens

programme.title: Crazy Delicious
programme.desc: Jayde Adams welcomes a molecular gastronomist, a fairytale lover and a comfort food fan into the culinary challenge. She sets them the tasks of making an unusual tomato-based dish and putting their own spin on a chicken pie to impress judges Heston Blumenthal, Niklas Ekstedt and Carla Hall. After these first two rounds, one of the contestants will be eliminated, leaving the remaining two to undertake the last test for the chance to win the golden apple - preparing afternoon tea

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Boca Raton in Florida to turn around the fortunes of Italian restaurant Anna Vincenzo's. programme.title: Car S.O.S.

Romantic drama, starring Joaquin Phoenix, Amy Adams and Rooney Mara, with the voice of Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Arthur's Hallowed Ground (1986)
programme.desc: A cricket groundsman plans to make the perfect pitch, but soon finds himself fighting a losing battle against his home team, who seem hell-bent on starting a turf war. Comedy drama, starring Jimmy Jewel, David Swift, Vas Blackwood and Michael Elphick

programme.title: Sunshine on Leith (2013)
programme.desc: Two soldiers return home to the Scottish port town after finishing their tour of duty in Afghanistan. While they struggle to adjust to life as civilians, the parents of one of the squaddies prepare to celebrate their 30th anniversary in style. Musical featuring songs by the Proclaimers, starring George MacKay, Kevin Guthrie, Peter Mullan and Jane Horrocks

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio

programme.title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer (2007)
programme.desc: The super-powered team takes on a mysterious alien who has been sent by his planet-eating master to prepare Earth for obliteration. While the four heroes try to find a way to foil its plans, an old enemy returns, intent on seizing the extraterrestrial's power. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Michael Chiklis, Chris Evans and Jessica Alba, with the voice of Laurence Fishburne

programme.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past (2014)
programme.desc: In a future ravaged by war, the superhero team makes a desperate last stand against a force of deadly robots. Their only hope is to send Wolverine back in time to the 1970s, to join forces with an earlier version of the X-Men and change the events that started the conflict. Superhero adventure sequel uniting the casts of the original movie and prequel First Class, starring Hugh Jackman, Jennifer Lawrence, James McAvoy and Michael Fassbender

programme.title: Central Intelligence (2016)
programme.desc: Action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson as a one-time geek who suffered constant humiliation at high school but grew up to be a lethal CIA agent. But when he is targeted by his own organisation, he comes home for a high school reunion, where he enlists the help of the former campus tough guy - now a meek accountant - who very quickly wonders what he has got himself into. Co-starring Kevin Hart and Amy Ryan

programme.title: Little Fockers (2010)
programme.desc: Nurse Greg is now the proud father of twins, but has yet to earn the respect of his wife's stern father after 10 years of marriage. The couple invite both sides of their extended family to the kids' birthday party, where Greg makes a desperate effort to finally prove his worth to his father-in-law. Comedy sequel, with Ben Stiller, Robert De Niro, Owen Wilson and Dustin Hoffman

programme.title: Men & Chicken (2015)
programme.desc: Two brothers discover they are adopted, and go in search of their biological father, a reclusive scientist. The search leads them to their three eccentric half-brothers, who live together on an island, and a shocking revelation about their father's research. Comedy, starring David Dencik and Mads Mikkelsen. In Danish

programme.title: Two Thousand Women (1944)
programme.desc: Female prisoners struggle to cope with living in a German internment camp, but find common cause in making life difficult for their captors. When three British airmen are shot down over the prison, they keep them hidden from the Germans while plotting a way to smuggle them out safely. Second World War comedy, with Phyllis Calvert, Flora Robson, Patricia Roc and Renee Houston

programme.title: Pimpernel Smith (1941)
programme.desc: An unassuming English professor working on an archaeological dig in Germany leads a double life helping enemies of the Nazi regime escape the country. Second World War adventure based on The Scarlet Pimpernel, directed by and starring Leslie Howard, with Francis L Sullivan, Mary Morris, Hugh McDermott, Raymond Huntley and Manning Whiley

programme.title: Man in the Saddle (1951)
programme.desc: A wealthy and ambitious cattleman plans to seize all the land surrounding his own. A peaceful rancher living nearby ignores warnings to sell up and avoid a conflict, and as he suffers a campaign of intimidation, he is forced to adopt violent methods to defend his livelihood. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Alexander Knox, Joan Leslie and Ellen Drew

programme.title: Sea of Sand (1958)
programme.desc: Allied troops fighting in North Africa in 1943 are redeployed as saboteurs behind enemy lines to blow up Rommel's desert fuel dumps. A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball

programme.title: Nutty Professor II: The Klumps (2000)
programme.desc: Bumbling scientist Sherman Klump is engaged to be married and determined to rid himself of his troublesome alter ego Buddy Love for ever. All his problems look set to be solved by his fiancee's latest DNA experiment - but things are about to spiral out of control. Comedy sequel, starring Eddie Murphy in multiple roles, along with Janet Jackson and Larry Miller

programme.title: The Other Woman (2014)
programme.desc: A woman is devastated to learn her boyfriend is actually married, but when she meets his wife, they quickly become friends. When they discover he has a third secret lover, they decide to join forces and teach him a lesson. Comedy, starring Cameron Diaz, Leslie Mann, Kate Upton and Nikolaj Coster-Waldau

programme.title: Miss Sloane (2016)
programme.desc: Elizabeth Sloane is the most sought after and formidable lobbyist in Washington DC. But when taking on the most powerful opponent of her career, she finds winning may come at too high a price. Drama, starring Jessica Chastain, Mark Strong and Gugu Mbatha-Raw

programme.title: A Pigeon Sat on a Branch Reflecting on Existence (2014)
programme.desc: Two bickering travelling salesmen wander the country, trying and failing to sell a range of joke shop products and having a series of strange encounters with people who give them a taste of the darker side of human existence. Comedy, starring Holger Andersson and Nils Westblom. In Swedish and English

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay goes back to two restaurants - Giuseppi's and Sante La Brea - to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use. Will he be pleased with their progress? Plus, an update on Finn McCool's from season one

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: Supernanny US
programme.desc: Jo Frost offers advice to the Garcias, with stay-at-home dad Anthony struggling to understand his new role as primary caregiver and Bethanie unable to find a work/life balance

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Nathan loves music as well as his home town of Stoke-on-Trent. So, when bride-to-be Beccie gives him free rein to plan their wedding, he decides to honour them - and not her - with nuptials based on the rare grooves of Northern Soul. Will this groom be able to deliver a swinging day, or will his bride be swinging wildly at him?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Gavin attempts to pull off a Thai-themed wedding in Somerset, while his fiancee Sophie dreams of a rustic, romantic affair

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Paris reveals some distressing news, and Verity decides that she must confront Charlie about it. Sam and Tiff enjoy becoming closer friends, much to the amusement of Reza and Alex

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present this year's semi-final, the last four bakers facing three patisserie challenges to decide who will be going through to next week's final. The signature round features plenty of alcohol, while the technical challenge sets the bakers the task of making pastry horns. Finally, for the Showstopper, they must use all the precision they can muster to construct a cake made entirely from smaller cakes

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Eloise works on a commission for a gravity-defying five-tier cake that will hang upside down, while Phil and Christine create a seven-foot-long Wallace and Gromit cake

programme.title: Epic (2013)
programme.desc: A teenager wanders into a forest that her scientist father believes is home to a race of miniature people. She stumbles on the tiny creatures and is shrunk down to their size so she can carry out a quest to save their new queen from an invading army. Animated adventure, with the voices of Amanda Seyfried, Josh Hutcherson, Colin Farrell and Christoph Waltz

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry attempts to drive a wedge between Adam and Pops when they debate the merits of a recently released Batman movie. Erica deliberately makes a mess of her interview at one university so that she will only receive an offer from her first choice of college, but her plan goes badly awry. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey, Sean Giambrone and Hayley Orrantia

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray is honoured as one of the area's top businessmen, Beverly's attempt to help her husband find a hobby backfires, and Erica comes to the rescue when Barry's scheme to steal golf carts goes awry

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is forced to consider how far he is willing to push himself in the name of science when a wealthy benefactor makes an indecent proposal. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, with Jessica Walter (Arrested Development) and Joshua Malina (The West Wing)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard is torn between his relationship with Bernadette and pleasing his mother - and comes up with a solution that leaves everyone unsatisfied. Leonard rekindles his romance with Raj's sister Priya, while Amy uses Penny's emotions as the basis for a research project

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon decides to make a new set of friends as he is depressed that the group's social world revolves around Leonard rather than himself. programme.desc: Sheldon is distraught when his online gaming account is hacked, so his friends decide to find out who is responsible. However, they make the disastrous mistake of confronting the culprit in person. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Mayim Bialik and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard decides to tell his mother that he and Bernadette are engaged - but she later collapses, making him fear the shock of the news has caused her to suffer a heart attack. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Laid-back groom Courtney plans to throw a wedding for his controlling bride-to-be Lauren at the caravan park she goes to every year

programme.desc: Two US couples from different backgrounds temporarily switch spouses as they navigate emotive issues such as politics, class, gender and race with the ultimate goal of everyone being able to find some common ground. here, the Benners and the McMichaels take part, as a perfectionist wife whose children act like grown-ups swaps places with a stay-at-home dad who thinks life is all about play

Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard inadvertently gives the impression he is attracted to Amy when he accompanies her to a colleague's wedding. Meanwhile, Howard tries to persuade Bernadette they should live with his mother after getting married and suggests a trial run of the arrangement over the weekend

programme.desc: The living exhibits at New York's Museum of Natural History are shipped to Washington's Smithsonian Institution - along with the artefact that brought them to life in the first place. Nightwatchman Larry tries to retrieve it, only to find the contents of the world's largest museum have been reanimated, including a pharaoh determined to take over the world. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Ben Stiller, Amy Adams, Owen Wilson and Steve Coogan

programme.title: The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey (2012)
programme.desc: Unassuming hobbit Bilbo Baggins is pulled out of his comfortable life when Gandalf the wizard volunteers him for a dangerous quest. He joins a party of dwarves who are on a mission to reclaim their lost homeland, which has been taken over by a dragon. When the group journeys into the Misty Mountains, Bilbo becomes separated from the dwarves and falls into a cave, where he finds himself confronted by Gollum and forced to play a riddle game. It is set in Middle-earth 60 years before the events of The Lord of the Rings and is the first part of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Martin Freeman, Ian McKellen, Richard Armitage, James Nesbitt, Ken Stott, Cate Blanchett, Ian Holm and Christopher Lee

Will Naked Attraction's unique dating method help him overcome his shyness?

However, he soon changes his mind when he finds out that Star Trek: The Next Generation actor Brent Spiner will also be there

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's mother comes to visit and woos the gang, but he cannot help feeling jealous of all the attention she is receiving - until a bout of illness lays him low, and he suddenly sees the benefits of having her around. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons

programme.title: Taskmaster

programme.desc: Sheldon is uninterested in Amy's career, so Penny, Raj and Leonard try to make him understand that sometimes it benefits a relationship to pretend to care about a partner's achievements. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.desc: For most people, living in a lighthouse is pure fantasy. Not so for Edward and Hazel Short and their two young daughters. Back in 2012, they set out to build a brand new, art deco, shining white lighthouse tower with additional bedrooms, guest rooms, and infinity pool on the side - all on a rugged but beautiful clifftop in North Devon. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary catching up with London's Crossrail engineers less than two years after the project's excavations uncovered the 14th-century Charterhouse plague pit. In an extraordinary turn of events, further work on the Liverpool Street station has revealed another burial ground, this time accommodating the capital's radicals, rebels and outsiders. The Bedlam burial ground, which is thought to be the final resting place of nearly 20,000 people, was believed to be lost for around 300 years. Now, with the excavation process under way, archaeologists and historians have been using bone analysis techniques in conjunction with parish records to reveal the names and life stories of those whose remains were found on-site. Their research reveals that people came from as far afield as Africa to work in the English capital, and sheds light on how pollution first became one of the city's most prolific killers

programme.title: Building Giants

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star in the domestic comedy which sees the couple appear to go from one disaster to the next, while coping with their three kids, Lucy's disapproving parents, Lee's feckless dad Frank and their world-weary friends Toby and Anna. programme.desc: The chef continues his exploration of the Far East's most diverse food cultures, travelling to Penang, Malaysia, to sample its multifaceted cuisine, which includes Malay, Indian, Chinese and British flavours. programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Reluctant tourist Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Matt Lucas for a frenetic 48 hours in the Italian capital. Their first challenge is finding suitable headgear to protect from the scorching Italian sun. They then take a guided tour in vintage Fiat 500 cars, and after wending their way past the Circus Maximus finish atop Gianicolo Hill in time for the cannon being fired - a daily ritual since the 1850s. Next they head to the border of the Vatican, before experiencing the very best of modern Italian cuisine. Day two sees visits to the Trevi Fountain and the Colosseum, perhaps Rome's most incredible antiquity

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On his journey from Swindon to Penzance, Michael Portillo explores Torquay, where he meets meteorologist David Braine to find out whether the town is Britain's warmest spot. He then takes a trip on the Paignton and Dartmouth Steam Railway, and spends the night at the Royal Castle Hotel in Dartmouth, where he learns about the town's naval past. After sailing up the River Dart he arrives in Totnes, and discovers the community's efforts to become more environmentally friendly

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo spends the night in a converted post office railway carriage before continuing his journey from Swindon to Penzance, heading for Par, where he meets miner Ivor Bowditch to explore the largest clay mine in the world. In Mevagissey he discovers how the methods for pilchard fishing have changed over the years, and on a visit to the Lost Gardens of Heligan, Michael learns about the 1,000-acre estate's history

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo undertakes the last leg of his journey from Swindon to Penzance. He travels to Perran Sands, where he searches for the lost church of St Piran, a place believed to be one of the earliest Christian sites in England. In Redruth, he explores the remains of the Cornish tin industry, before heading to St Ives, where he spends the night in Treganna Castle. The former politician finishes his trip in Penzance, discovering how railways link remote communities to the rest of the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey to London from Buxton, Derbyshire, where he discovers how railways inspired a building craze and boosted tourism. In the Peak District, he joins a team of volunteers repairing damage done to ancient peat landscapes during the Industrial Revolution, and near the quarries of Stanton Moor he learns how trains transported Derbyshire's famous sandstone all over England

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey from Buxton to London, stopping at Cromford, Derbyshire, where Bradshaw's railway guide reports Richard Arkwright built his original cotton mill. He also visits Derby to take a walk through Britain's first public park, and heads for Burton-on-Trent for a tour of Burton Bridges Breweries

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey from Buxton to London continues in Walsall, where he visits a leather business that still supplies the Queen with saddlery. He then explores how the centre of Birmingham has changed since Bradshaw's times, and is taught how to cook a curry by the owner of a leading Indian restaurant. Finally, he heads to the village of Bournville, where he learns how the railway transformed the fortunes of the Cadbury brothers

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailand's forests, fertile plains and even its city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people, and spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nation's rice bowl - but even here, there are magical places to be found. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of Northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: A profile of Dudley Mason, who was awarded the George Cross after the tanker he was commanding came under attack during Operation Pedestal in August 1942 to relieve Malta

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: An examination of how Leon Degrelle and an array of Finns, Estonians and fellow Belgians joined German volunteer brigades to hold off the Red Army on the Eastern front during the Second World War and enabled 56,000 men to escape

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi official's death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An eerie, empty country village which hides a sad secret, a mysterious tower battered by the elements and a disused railway deep beneath the streets of a city

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A settlement in Argentina destroyed by floods, a town in the US that has been erased, and a Soviet dream-town that turned into a nuclear nightmare

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A seemingly endless concrete line stretching through the French countryside, which sits as a monument to a discarded engineering advancement

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene and his staff disguise themselves as undertakers in a bid to rescue the British airmen. Meanwhile, Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen decide they want the pilots to join the Gestapo and dress up as German officers - but are arrested before they get chance to put their plan into action

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle concocts another plan to infiltrate German headquarters and rescue the British airmen using hijacked German uniforms, but who will they fit?

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Cutting-edge investigations at the Bermuda Triangle might confirm the newest theories of what really goes on in this deadly stretch of water

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some 'emerging' sporting classics from the Far East when three Datsun Zs appear, and an unusual lot arrives that run on caterpillar tracks not wheels

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: Artefacts for sale at auction with connections to William Shakespeare and a Nazi fighter pilot, as well as rare dinosaur skeletons and a tool used to design the city of Bath

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: Artefacts for sale at auction are examined to reveal their historical significance, including items linked to German warships, vampire mythology, and Queen Victoria's coronation

programme.desc: The work carried out by ONE Columba, which is capable of carrying 14,000 shipping containers, but has the dangerous challenge of navigating twisting rivers and ancient ports

programme.title: Monster Ships

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson joins an unprecedented archaeological expedition to Belgium in search of a perfectly preserved WWI bunker called the Vampire.

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi official's death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The underground base with the power to destroy a nation, the site linked with an attempt to kill a vicious dictator, and an ancient world discovered in the open American wilderness

programme.title: Moon Landings: World's Greatest Hoax?
programme.desc: Examining the most famous conspiracy theories behind the 1969 Apollo space program that saw Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walk on the moon - or did they?

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Kirby Hall in Northamptonshire, where members of the public bring along treasured antiques and collectibles to hear their stories and find out their worth. Items include a jade bowl once used as a butter dish, marine paintings from the family of Captain Bligh, a vintage TV set with a four-inch screen, a medieval ring found with the aid of a metal detector and a wind-up Charlie Chaplin doll. In the new feature looking back at previous items, art specialist Philip Mould hopes to learn more about a 500-year-old religious painting, originally featured in 2010 and now fully restored

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The small, picturesque seaside station at Dawlish in Devon is brought to life by an influx of people attending an air show, and the company lays on extra staff, schedules more trains and employs different queuing systems to cope with all the passengers. At Bath Spa, gate-line operator Peter Eagle tries his hand at foreign languages as he directs international tourists to and from the ancient city

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: Nazi Hunters

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide as he travels through Spain and Portugal. He begins in the city of La Coruna, where he examines the Celtic roots of the Galician people and tries to master the bagpipes, before meeting walkers from all over the world on the pilgrims' trail to Santiago de Compostela. Heading across the border to Porto, Michael finds out about the origins of Britain's long alliance with the Portuguese, and finishes his journey in the capital Lisbon

With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a photographer to find a new home on the Greek island of Skopelos in time for his 60th birthday, showcasing five properties on a budget of £120,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mette and Chris from Brighton want a holiday home on Majorca, so Scarlette Douglas lines up five properties for them and their two children on the north coast of the island

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Chris and Kay from Buckinghamshire are searching for their perfect retirement home to share with their two cats. The only hitch is that they can't agree on where it should be. Kay loves the stunning scenery of Cardigan Bay in west Wales, while sun-worshipper Chris would rather move to the beautiful Mar Menor in Spain. Jasmine Harman selects three properties for their £150,000 budget in Wales, but Jonnie Irwin has three cracking options in the Mar Menor

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows five properties to Pat and David, who have been married for 44 years and are selling up in Northampton to move to Portugal's Silver Coast

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Flamangos restaurant in Whitehouse, New Jersey, where he tries to help the mother-and-daughter owners turn around their struggling business's fortunes. He faces a struggle because parent Adelle has a bad relationship with her staff and is disappointed by the resident chef, leaving Gordon with the added challenge of changing her attitude

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay gives his advice to Paul Bazzini, an award-winning chef from New York who now owns a restaurant in Ridgewood, New Jersey. Paul confesses he has lost his passion for food and is struggling to keep up with the business, a situation that seems evident to other members of staff, his wife and customers

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Mojito, a Cuban cafe in Brooklyn, New York. The restaurant was the shared dream of two newlyweds who have separated since its opening, and whose constant public quarrelling is distracting staff and driving customers away

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits PJ's Steakhouse in Queens, New York, to provide his expert advice to the owners. They decided to turn a successful bar into a high-end restaurant, but without any previous experience in managing a business they have found themselves seriously in debt. With an over-confident chef and a partner propping up the bar, co-owner Madalyn faces a few difficult decisions

programme.title: Surviving the Stone Age
programme.desc: The tribe's newly appointed lead hunter makes his first kill, a large male deer, after several days spent stalking the forest. The much-needed meat is soon being preserved to keep the tribe going, while the skin and bones are used to make more tools. Some downtime provides the experts with the opportunity to make rock-face art

programme.title: Educating Greater Manchester
programme.desc: The pressure is on at Harrop Fold as GCSE exams get underway, and the school finds itself hitting the headlines when four members of staff are suspended over allegations of removing disadvantaged and struggling pupils from the school roll to improve exam results. The new heads girl launches an online campaign against uniform policy that lands her in trouble. Mr Walker is concerned that year 10 pupil Morgan may not be able to catch up with Spanish in time for her exams, and Mr Povey intervenes to find out the underlying cause of her disruptive behaviour

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present this year's semi-final, the last four bakers facing three patisserie challenges to decide who will be going through to next week's final. The signature round features plenty of alcohol, while the technical challenge sets the bakers the task of making pastry horns. Finally, for the Showstopper, they must use all the precision they can muster to construct a cake made entirely from smaller cakes

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand presents the companion show to the baking contest, inviting guests Joe Wilkinson, Jordan Banjo and Prue Leith to reflect on Patisserie Week. Regular contributor Tom Allen waves his whisk at the socially distanced home bakers and their tasty treats, while Jo introduces unseen footage and an exclusive interview with the baker leaving the competition. The host also unearths the delights and the frights being baked in viewers' kitchens

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: With autumn just around the corner, it's a chance for the Strawbridge family to make the most of the wild larder on their doorstep. Dick is determined to make sloe gin, while Angel presses on with her plan to create a library in the pepper pot tower

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Following Sarah Beeny and her family as they relocate from London to Somerset, renovating a semi-derelict former dairy farm into their dream family home. In the second episode, Sarah and her husband Graham await news from the local council, while the family get to work planting 1,000 young trees they have bought to improve the biodiversity on the farm

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts visits Portsmouth to investigate the history of the Royal Navy, uncovering some uncomfortable truths behind the myth surrounding Lord Horatio Nelson and getting first-hand experience of what life would have been like in the heat of battle at Trafalgar. She also visits an archaeological dig on Burrow Island and a lantern show at the New Theatre Royal, and discovers the brutal punishments doled out for even minor offences by the Victorian penal system

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Personal assistant Lisa hosts the first of five parties from in and around Birmingham, hoping her Malaysian-themed menu will impress other contestants Akil, Sonia, Martha and Mark

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mark hosts the third night of the culinary competition taking place in Birmingham, hoping to impress his guests with a menu of classic comfort food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Youth worker Sonia is in charge on day four in Birmingham and after three nights of vegan chat from Martha, the host makes a pact with the others to ban any mention of the subject

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra wonders how Ray would cope if she met an untimely end, and picks an attractive friend to replace her as his new wife - but cannot help being hurt by his enthusiastic response to the idea

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Two years ago, George meets Armin and Amifa, whose dark and dingy 1920s house in Bellingham, south east London, was not just ugly - the DIY extension added by former owners had been condemned and the makeshift kitchen was an accident waiting to happen. Stewart and Iain confront a woman in Cheshire who is refusing to pay fees to a veterinary practice, claiming her dog has been misdiagnosed, while Paul and Max collect £5,000 owed by a banned company director in east London accused of mis-selling assets on the stock market

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gary and Paul visit Kent to recover £2,500 owed by a publican to a utilities supplier - only to find themselves in the middle of a family at war. Stewart and Elmor deal with a sensitive case in Liverpool, as they repossess a property where the tenant has failed to pay any rent for almost a year

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments. Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriff Paul Bohill tackles the case of a small debt owed by ex-footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock to a kennel owner, before being forced to evict a school dinner lady in south London. Meanwhile, in the north of England enforcement agent Stewart MacCracken heads out with trainee Iain Taylor to confront a Yorkshire builder they need to get payment from at his home, while a reptile shop owner hit by online competitors urgently needs to pay a debt for the feed company he relies on to keep his animals alive

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drug dealer who has been driving dangerously reveals a surprising amount of information when he is pulled over, and friends try to baffle the pursuit team by changing their names. Meanwhile, an officer has a shock in store for a law-breaking driver

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: An officer is nearly run over by a desperate driver, and another's patience is tested by a drunken motorist. Plus, a law enforcer goes in search of a suspicious character, and a group of youths tries to deceive the interception unit

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: It is Halloween and Jane spots Dipi kissing a costumed man she believes to be Shane, while Harlow sets aside her anger when she realises that Hendrix might be leaving

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell

Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Fisher Stevens and Molly Hagan

Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, George Hamilton and Lesley Warren

Meanwhile, Bishop and Jake discuss their marital problems. Naval crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

Naval crime drama, guest starring Scott Bakula

The DIY expert enlists guest designer Oliver Heath and more than 100 local traders to add an extension, reconfigure the property's layout and transform the garden in a bid to help the pair move on with their lives

programme.title: Cornwall Air 999
programme.desc: The work of pilots and paramedics of Cornwall Air Ambulance as they race to save lives of holiday-makers and locals across one of Britain's busiest counties

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper

programme.title: Embarrassing Bodies
programme.desc: Pixie McKenna helps a young patient with a six-year posterior problem that started with a tiny hair but ended up as a gaping hole. Christian Jessen advises Dawn, a transitioning male-to-female transsexual who has spent a lifetime in a body that didn't feel right, while in Magaluf, the boys clock up the alcohol count and it's time to check for liver damage. The truck rolls up in Newcastle to find a man with a worrying lump and a lady whose hair loss means her confidence needs rebuilding. Plus, Pixie demonstrates to local girls what smoking can do to the face

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: Bedtime snacks begin disappearing, so Zigby investigates

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra invents a game that allows everyone to join in

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack tries to save the village from a swarm of ravenous butterflies, but ends up accidentally causing a volcanic eruption

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The boys must find a way to make it rain, but Jack falls under the spell of a flying mermaid

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: The geese fly over a glacial region, and find themselves in the middle of erupting geysers

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Krobak steals Nils' propeller for his master to knock down the Gem of Destiny

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: The story of the musical, an art form that emerged from vaudeville, operetta, burlesque and the British music hall. Featuring a script by Stephen Sondheim

programme.title: Operation Third Form (1966)
programme.desc: A trio of youngsters tries to stop a gang of crooks and save an old junk collector from being accused of theft. Children's drama, starring Kevin Bennett and Sydney Bromley

programme.title: The October Man (1947)
programme.desc: A chemist with suicidal tendencies is falsely accused of murder - and his precarious mental state is further upset by taunts from the real killer. Psychological thriller, adapted from a novel by Eric Ambler, starring John Mills, Joan Greenwood and Joyce Carey

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Take My Life (1947)
programme.desc: A devoted wife journeys to Scotland in a bid to prove her husband has been wrongly accused of murdering his former girlfriend. Thriller, with Greta Gynt, Hugh Williams, Marius Goring and Francis L Sullivan

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises British house-hunters looking for a place in Crete, showing them round a traditional Greek home, a modern village property and an old Venetian-style farm, each for less than £60,000

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps Wiltshire couple and pop opera fanatics Nikki Kedge and Angela Rawson find a bigger home in their dream location of Scotland's west coast. Desperate for more room to put on plays for local children, they view six houses in the county of Dumfries and Galloway - but will there be anything that tickles their fancy?

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A distressed teenager explores cures for hair-loss, and a professional singer tries to save her career by learning to control her severe acid reflux. A mother fears her six-year-old son will be a target for bullying because of his psoriasis, and doctors help an obese father reverse damage done to his liver

programme.title: Europe at Sea (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the highest echelons of the European Union and their reaction to a changing world order

programme.title: The Orchard
programme.desc: On a small Caribbean island, a Rastafarian and two colleagues are nurturing a plot of land to grow exotic foods despite tough conditions and the devastating effects of hurricanes

programme.title: Abraham's Point (2008)
programme.desc: A man who left his childhood home in Wales 15 years previously decides to face up to his troubled past. He steals a grandfather clock and takes it on a journey across the country, intending to return the vintage timepiece to his father, who is dying. Drama, starring Mackenzie Crook and Joseph Milson

programme.title: My Saviour (2019)
programme.desc: A woman whose life is in disarray is attacked by a local gang, only for a mysterious stranger to come to her rescue. She forms an intense relationship with her rescuer, but their close bond sends her on a disturbing journey into a nightmarish world of violence and abuse. Drama, starring Bayley Freer and Steven Murphy

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: A south London couple gamble their savings in an attempt to raise enough cash to buy their dream home in the countryside. The plan is to renovate their two-bedroom former council flat so they can make money by renting it out for as much as possible. Property developer Sarah Beeny keeps an eye on their progress

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Kenny ropes the lads into helping him build a hi-tech guinea pig hutch complete with swimming pool, while Mike extends the hand of hospitality to a temporarily homeless Rex - who promptly takes over his entire flat and has the temerity to invite a few friends along for good measure. Last in series, starring Martin Freeman, Ken Morley and Peter Serafinowicz

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists arrive in San Sebastian, where bad weather forces them to stay indoors. Meanwhile, Brendan investigates a coach romance and one of the passengers gets a makeover

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps Wiltshire couple and pop opera fanatics Nikki Kedge and Angela Rawson find a bigger home in their dream location of Scotland's west coast. Desperate for more room to put on plays for local children, they view six houses in the county of Dumfries and Galloway - but will there be anything that tickles their fancy?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises a couple looking for a holiday home in Cyprus for under £70,000, weighing up the pros and cons of a mountain lodge, a 200-year-old village house and a luxury villa

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a woman and her best friend search for properties along the North Yorkshire coast in Whitby, Filey and Scarborough, where their budget of £265,000 can buy them some impressive homes, which need to have enough room for a pet duck, goose and two chickens

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: A couple hope to swap the grime and grind of east London for a healthy life on the Pembrokeshire coast. Seetha Hallett shows them six properties in Tenby, Pembroke and the unique village of Saundersfoot, where rehearsals for the D-Day landings took place in 1943. But will their budget of £300,000 find a property big enough to accommodate the duo, some foster children and their grandmother?

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Chris and Jeana Hardcastle from North Yorkshire are looking to move to Cornwall's south coast and are shown around six very different houses in three locations - the Lizard Peninsula, Falmouth Bay and Mousehole

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a recently retired couple from the Home Counties find a property on Ireland's Donegal coast. They are shown around six homes, including a traditional farmhouse and a bungalow with views over the Atlantic

programme.title: Pop Pirates (1984)
programme.desc: A pop group enters a talent contest, but their chances are jeopardised by the lead singer's involvement in video piracy. Drama, starring Spencer Chandler and Roger Daltrey

programme.title: Passport to Pimlico (1949)
programme.desc: The residents of a London suburb discover documents proving their neighbourhood is not part of Britain, and declare independence. Appointing their own head of state, they begin erecting borders as the government resorts to desperate measures to bring the community back under British rule. Ealing comedy, starring Stanley Holloway and Margaret Rutherford

programme.title: Maria Marten: Or, Murder in the Red Barn (1935)
programme.desc: An evil squire lures a woman into marriage, but when she becomes pregnant, he decides to murder her, leaving a young gypsy who is in love with her to take the blame. Period drama, starring Tod Slaughter and Sophie Stewart

programme.title: The Getting of Wisdom (1977)
programme.desc: A snobbish Victorian college for young ladies is thrown into disarray by the arrival of a strong-willed girl from the Outback. Australian drama, starring Barry Humphries, Susannah Fowle, John Waters and Terence Donovan

programme.title: I am Joseph
programme.desc: Short film about one man's internal struggle to come to terms with an unavoidable truth that will transform his life

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Newlyweds Peter and Sharon Casley are offered advice by Amanda Lamb as they search for a second home in Auvergne, France. They explore the potential of three unusual homes, choosing between a 12th-century chateau, an ancient fort and a property built into a rock face

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a Bristol-based family longing for the open spaces and breathtaking countryside offered by the east coast of Scotland. The hunt for their dream property takes them more than 60 miles along the coastline, visiting Dundee, renowned for its marmalade and 400-million-year-old volcano, the harbour town of Montrose and Stonehaven, home to Dunnottar Castle

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist is joined by fellow musicians for an impromptu jam session, as well as a chat about their lives in rock. In this edition he is joined by Alice Cooper

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the fate of the 'Dunlop' family of foxes in Essex as they fight for survival in the wild. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary

programme.title: Fingersmith
programme.desc: Dashing conman Rivers embarks on an audacious scheme to elope with heiress Maud and steal her fortune. He enlists the help of petty thief Sue who becomes the lady's maid to win her confidence - but as she develops a close friendship with her employer, her doubts about the plan increase. Drama, starring Rupert Evans, Elaine Cassidy, Sally Hawkins and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Hoodluck (2020)
programme.desc: In the heart of southeast London, winning the lottery rarely happens, but then three guys - Solse, Festa and Kevin - come across a 'Be Lucky' lottery ticket in a stolen handbag

programme.title: Brothers' Day (2015)
programme.desc: A Manchester teenager feels abandoned by his older brother, who has turned to a life of crime. However, when his latest deal ends badly, the younger sibling finds his eyes opened to the impossible choices his brother faces every day. Crime drama, starring Tom Collins and Lewis Fletcher

programme.title: Europe at Sea (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the highest echelons of the European Union and their reaction to a changing world order

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The coach reaches Poitiers on the French leg of the tour, where there is a chance to explore a chateau. The travellers also visit an animal park, before turning their attention to the forthcoming vote

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a Bristol-based family longing for the open spaces and breathtaking countryside offered by the east coast of Scotland. The hunt for their dream property takes them more than 60 miles along the coastline, visiting Dundee, renowned for its marmalade and 400-million-year-old volcano, the harbour town of Montrose and Stonehaven, home to Dunnottar Castle

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb takes a mother and daughter on a tour of tempting properties along Turkey's Turquoise Coast, including a huge mountain house with garden and sea views - a bargain at £42,000

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra plans a surprise for Bertie and McMeer

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebras watch shooting stars and McMeer decides he wants to have one of his own

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The phoenixes are migrating, so all the villagers are desperate to get their hands on a magic fiery feather to keep them warm through the winter

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi are sucked into the Book of Tales, where they encounter a mixed-up monster and a knight who has lost his sword and shield

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Re-joining the flock, Nils and Martin are caught by a group of crows who have come to join the Black Elf

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Smirre repeatedly attacks the geese, who only just manage to escape

programme.title: Skippy The Bush Kangaroo
programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Norwich couple Simon and Caitlin embark on an ambitious project under the watchful eye of expert Sarah Beeny. They plan to buy a former council house, invest all their savings and completely renovate it in the hope that a subsequent sale will raise enough money to move onto the next rung of the property ladder

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb presents the best properties she found on her real-estate tour of Florida and the Caribbean in the show that aims to advise British househunters looking for a second home abroad

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: Following the fate of the 'Dunlop' family of foxes in Essex as they fight for survival in the wild. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A woman with a seven-month-old puppy faces a difficult decision when Noel Fitzpatrick reveals he must perform a risky operation to treat the young dog's bone deformities. Two animals injured in road accidents are rushed into the surgery and a Labrador puts its newly transplanted hip to the test when it is reunited with its owner

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Brockley, Peckham and Rotherhithe. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Davies advises a couple from Cornwall looking for a home in the western part of Portugal's picturesque Algarve. They are hoping their £100,000 budget will buy them a property set in the area's rolling landscapes and beautiful beaches

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett selects memorable moments from the house-hunting series as she returns to some of her favourite locations. From Woolacombe to Whitby, and Margate to the Isle of Wight, the retrospective illustrates why the English coastline has become the latest property hotspot

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the residents for a year of their lives

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves

programme.title: Murder Investigation Team
programme.desc: The team are called to a lake where the body of a vagrant has been found - and within minutes discover a second body, that of a teacher, has been pushed from his fifth-floor window. Despite the very different circumstances, the investigators still suspect there's a link. Lindsey Coulson and Samantha Spiro star in the gritty police procedural drama, with a guest appearance by John Duttine

programme.title: IBA Boxing
programme.desc: IBA Boxing brings you the best of the adrenaline pumping fights and knock out action from their legendary events.

programme.title: My Saviour (2019)
programme.desc: A woman whose life is in disarray is attacked by a local gang, only for a mysterious stranger to come to her rescue. She forms an intense relationship with her rescuer, but their close bond sends her on a disturbing journey into a nightmarish world of violence and abuse. Drama, starring Bayley Freer and Steven Murphy

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises British house-hunters looking for a place in Crete, showing them round a traditional Greek home, a modern village property and an old Venetian-style farm, each for less than £60,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Newlyweds Peter and Sharon Casley are offered advice by Amanda Lamb as they search for a second home in Auvergne, France. They explore the potential of three unusual homes, choosing between a 12th-century chateau, an ancient fort and a property built into a rock face

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Brockley, Peckham and Rotherhithe. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Total Wipeout
programme.desc: Twenty more contestants compete, tackling four physically demanding games on the purpose-built obstacle course in Argentina. Only three participants will make it through to the Wipeout Zone, where they will face a 45ft slide, rolling logs, a climbing wall, a dizzying spinning plate and a pair of trampolines in their efforts to win £10,000. Presented by Richard Hammond and Amanda Byram

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Gym instructor Chris thinks he can use his fitness skills to win big, while student Sophie hopes she can outsmart The Cube. Both contestants try to complete a series of increasingly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £250,000. Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Teacher Sarah dreams of climbing Mount Kilimanjaro, but first she must take on the mighty Cube, while pizza delivery man Tony reckons he's got what it takes to beat it. Tackling a series of increasingly difficult challenges, the contestants are under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £250,000. Phillip Schofield hosts

The House That £100K Built presenter Kieran Long teams up with fellow architecture specialist Piers Taylor, while Homes Under the Hammer host Martin Roberts joins forces with Selling Houses' Amanda Lamb.
